Sir Percival Lateef only had a short moment of wonder, before the horror struck. The beach he had arrived on was surrounded by cliffs, meaning his only options were to climb or to swim, and since he was wearing the heavy armour of the White Knights, he decided that sinking wasn’t an option.

It took him many hours and several near falls, but he eventually made his way up and down the cliffside, only to encounter a region filled with the ravenous undead.

As a White Knight he trained to fight such evils and he fended them off as best he could, yet there were too many for him to face alone. He was forced to retreat, to a shrine, atop a rocky island, displaying the symbol of Saradomin, and there he prayed.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Trapped by a shrine that stood atop a rocky island, surrounded by the hungry dead, Sir Lateef prayed to Saradomin with all his strength. He begged for salvation, for a miracle that might free him, but silence was his only answer.

He barricaded himself atop the monument, the dead scrabbling at his defences, their unholy growling keeping him from even a moment’s sleep.

As time drew on, and hunger and thirst took him, he turned to the symbol of Saradomin in defeated rage. Where was his god? Why was he abandoned? Had he not spent his life in service of the lord of light?

Enraged, he took his sword and cleaved Saradomin’s symbol from its plinth, causing it to crash to the floor. Filled with guilt, he hid the fallen piece from his sight. His last act was one of blasphemy, but perhaps an act justly performed.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Wild Knights were known for their ability to scout the wild areas of Ashenfall. They could walk where no one else dared to tread, and they were masters of the hunt, putting even the garou to shame.

Their archers wore sturdy leathers, using carefully laid folds to shield them against harm. They would break easily, but the archers made it part of their ritual to maintain them regularly. Each day they would wake and tend to the leather, making their armour seem brand new with each morning.

Archers of the Wild Knights were quick, silent and deadly. A joke their more melee focused companions used to raise on those rare occasions that the knights would gather. Drink and company lead to banter and poor jokes, which created much missed laughter that the Wild Knights always enjoyed.

Archer, warrior or mage, the Wild Knights would hurl jibes at once another, without a moment’s thought. Yet, when needed, there was no stronger camaraderie and they would all die for one another.
}}

Their archers wore sturdy leathers, using carefully laid folds to shield them against harm. They would break easily, but the archers made it part of their ritual to maintain them regularly. Each day they would wake and tend to the leather, making their armour seem brand new with each morning.

Archers worked alone, it was easier that way. They could stalk their prey, silent and slowly, sometimes taking days between kills as they followed their quarry around the lands. They would hunt monsters and people with equal focus, as dictated by the order’s commands.

To harm the wilds was to make yourself a target for the Wild Knights. To sully the green lands of Guthix, an unforgivable crime. Yet, there were always those who would risk their ire, wannabe despots, and greedy fools yearning to plunder the realm.

They all met the fate they deserved, in the end.

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
These blue robes are the signature clothing worn by every respectable mage. Apprentices look forward to the day they first wear these, as it shows they have graduated to full wizard status. The wizard's tower, in particular, uses these robes as a uniform, signifying their magical power and fealty to the god of order, Saradomin.

On Ashenfall, it was rare for those wearing these robes to be seen outside the wizard's guild. The structure on the hillside was home to strange practices and even stranger experiments. Powerful spells were studied and unleashed across an already magically problematic countryside.

The White Knights, often summoned to address the perils that the wizard's guild inadvertently unleashed, were a frequent presence. Their duties ranged from quelling peculiar weather phenomena to managing the 'accidental' summoning of demons. To maintain control, a contingent of knights established a permanent residence within the guild, their vigilant eyes ever watchful.
}}

pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Zamorak Cape
|image = Zamorak Cape.png
|type = Armour
|description = A cape that proudly displays obeisance to the god Zamorak.
}}
'''Zamorak Cape''' is an armour item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Armour
|meleedefence = 2
|rangeddefence = 2
|magicdefence = 2
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Crafting Table
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 0
|output1 = Zamorak Cape
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Zamorak Cape}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Zamorak never came to Dowdun Reach. Its gates were never graced by the mahjarrat. No general came, or ambassador from the mainland. Even the ZMI failed to send an offshoot to the keep. 

How was that possible? Chaos taught them that, by simple laws of averages, a high-ranking Zamorakian should have stumbled onto their shores. Ashenfall and Dowdun Reach was too much of an opportunity. Greed must have led a Zamorakian highborn here. 

The silence was deafening. Everyone who lived or worked in the keep knew what this meant: Zamorak had barred his inner circle from visiting Ashenfall, likely at pain of death.  How else could Dowdun Reach have been left alone for so long?

But why? Why did Zamorak keep Ashenfall at arm's length? The answers differed, depending on the zeal of the follower. Zamorak feared something on Ashenfall. Zamorak knew that something made it worthless. Zamorak was already here, just without Dowdun's knowing. Zamorak is in all of us.

Remarkably, that mystery only strengthened the beliefs of Dowdun's Zamorakians. By being so contemptible to Zamorak, they were utterly important. They were main characters in Zamorak's performance. 

It suggests another reason why Zamorak might have left Ashenfall alone. Perhaps he wanted to show that nothing - not even his disdain - would soften his people's infatuation. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_armour_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

The downfall was sudden. They would not admit it, but the magi had lost control of their demons. Their reaction was the same as always: to deny and deflect. If you accused the magi of summoning demons outside of sanctioned areas, they would smirk and give a carousel of different answers: you are lying; you are mistaken; you are cowardly; you are to be eaten by demons. 

Something terrible was bound to happen; it was a question of when. An infernal demon materialised during a Shadow Knight training session. The recruits were memorising guard rotations, so only their teacher was armed. It was a massacre. 

Alric and Lord Rasmodel locked themselves in the war room to argue. None were to enter. Only the two of them know what was said - oh, to be a raven, watching - but everyone knows the result. 

The Dreaming Stone, and the allocation of power that came from it, would be managed solely by Lord Rasmodel. The demon portal would have its power rationed.

Alric and his magi had their wings clipped.
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Zamorak Robe Legs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

Before the re-routing of the Dreaming Stone's energy-flows, people weren't exposed to the energy tethers for long. But now that Dowdun Reach was a lattice of Dreaming Stone energy, that changed. The flows ran through people's rooms as they slept. It pulsed and hummed and bled.

People began to change. Dreams became rarities; nightmares the commonplace. People heard whispers in quiet moments. Through lack of both sleep and solace, the magi were frayed at the edges. They would be quick to anger.

Then the infestation came. People fell sick; a great wave moved from the magi wing outwards. The lucky were feverish or comatose, left to die on stretchers. The unlucky carried battles in their heads. Voices warred within them, and they would buckle over in pain as screaming headaches took them. 

The epidemic was so terrible, so consuming, that Lord Rasmodel's actions might be considered understandable. He was desperate, and likely ill himself. 

Lord Rasmodel shattered the Dreaming Stone with his warhammer. It smashed - its shards tossed to the winds. For one glorious moment, the voices stopped. Lord Rasmodel felt peace in that moment. But something worse was unleashed, and the horrors doubled.
}}

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Zamorak Robes}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

At first, the changes to Dowdun Reach were positive. By throttling the power from the Dreaming Stone, there were fewer incidents at the demon portal. Demons rarely appeared without being summoned, and those summonings were restricted to the portal room. If the Reach was once a lightning rod for demons, it was now grounded with only the occasional spark.

Alric was never going to accept this new status quo. His faithful began re-routing the power of the Dreaming Stone. Energy tethers from the Dreaming Stone passed through and round residences. The Library, which warded against magical overflows, had its power siphoned. 

Where the Dreaming Stone's energy-flows were once arterial, they were now like capillaries. The energy spread everywhere, a webbing throughout the Reach. And that had consequences. 

Some of those consequences were explosive, sudden. The capillaries would overload, leading to fires and outbreaks. The magi would blame the Shadow Knights for these discharges, since Lord Rasmodel was now steward of the Dreaming Stone. 

Other consequences were less sudden. They barely manifested at all, but their effects were insidious.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Arcane Infusion
|image = Arcane Infusion.png
|type = Consumable
|description = Puts the whiz in wizard.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}

==Recipe==

|output1 = Arcane Infusion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Arcane Infusion}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Arcane Infusion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Wizards always push themselves too far. They compete academically with anything that moves, insisting on demonstrating their intellectual superior over everyone around them. Their intelligence is a glittering brooch that must be seen by everyone.

Naturally, this sort of mentality causes many a wizard to burn out. Academia creates a sort of stress that could be bottled and weaponised, only to be banned by conventions around the world for its cruelty. Yet, a wizard does it to themselves, pushing and pushing beyond the limits of the human condition.

Inevitably they turn to external solutions, and this particular mixture has pushed many a mage past academic burnout just long enough to get by. Somehow it makes magic flow easier, spells cast stronger than before, and the wizard is capable of feats they might never have achieved before.

They also tell themselves that it lets them get by on only two hours of sleep a night. It does not and it all catches up with them in the end. Wizard burnout can be disturbingly literal.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Don’t eat them. No, seriously, don’t. At least not raw. Cadavaberries are poisonous and deadly, often used in sinister ceremonies of ritual sacrifice. The cadavaberries on Ashenfall are equally toxic, but can be coaxed into good behaviour with the judicious addition of fire.

The toxins can be burned away, providing a surprisingly pleasant tasting berry beneath. But, one must be careful, for preparing them wrong is the last mistake someone is likely to make.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Consumable/Magical Focus
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Magical Focus
|image = Magical Focus.png
|type = Consumable
|description = Imbue your staff to increase the damage of your magical attacks for a limited time.
|weight = 5.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Magical Focus''' is a consumable item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Crafting Table
|mat1 = Wild Anima
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Stone
|mat2qty = 10
|output1 = Magical Focus
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Magical Focus}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The wizard Mallory was never as good as he had hoped to be. He was proficient in spellcasting and his rote memory was excellent, but the power of his spells always fell a little short. He advanced through the ranks mostly through technicality, but he never quite earned the respect of his peers. 

That was until the day he created the focus. Mallory had always been good at making things. He enjoyed the simpler magic of crafting, and he found you could weave small threads of magic into the construction of something, breathing just a little bit more life and wonder into it than before. True craftsmanship, he believed, carried a little bit of the creator’s soul in it. 

The focus proved this theory and elevated it further. By creating a focus and subconsciously imbuing it with a bit of his essence, it would create a smoother, more fluid, channel of magic through the body, which created spells of greater potency. 

Tragically, after sharing his knowledge with the other wizards, he was murdered by his mentor. Who then took his knowledge and claimed it as his own. Karma is a stern judge, however, and the offending wizard later found himself being swallowed whole and digested by a dragon. 

Just punishment, I’m sure Mallory would agree.  
}}

It is tasty… but at what price?

{{Journal entry|
Popular among Wild Knight scouts, this mixture focussed the senses and improved reaction times. It was taken by hunters as they explored the dangerous forests of Ashenfall, allowing them respond to threats faster than before. Those extra moments saved countless lives.

In the village of Bramblemead there was a contingent of foolish youngsters who began drinking this mixture in large quantities, due to the energetic thrill it created. It would allow them to engage in strenuous activities for far longer without getting tired.

Its popularly began to grow among older members of the population as well, though no one would be willing to explain why.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Consumable/Small Wardstone
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Small Wardstone
|image = Small Wardstone.png
|type = Consumable
|description = Recharges your armour with a small amount of lifeward.
|weight = 5.0
|stacklimit = 25
}}
'''Small Wardstone''' is a consumable item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Rune Altar
|skill = Runecrafting
|mat1 = Stone
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Rune Essence
|mat2qty = 5
|output1 = Small Wardstone
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Small Wardstone}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Wardstones are handy tools for anyone working with wards. Armour, when created on an anima-rich continent like Ashenfall, is infused with threads of enchantment that harness natural magic to provide an extra layer of protection for the wearer.

This protection depletes upon use, as the energy within the ward is expended on the effect. But the wardstone can rectify this situation… for a price.

The stone around the wardstone is hollow and more brittle than it seems, and the rune essence is carefully laid within it along with a final ingredient – a drop of the creators own blood. This blood, and the rune essence formation, creates a sort of harmonious connection between the bearer of the wardstone and the stone itself.

When the stone is broken, it activates the rune essence and draws anima in from the nearest pure source – the soul of the user.

Souls are containers of pure anima. They grow over time from experience and adventure, the more the bearer of said soul enriches their life with activity, the stronger the soul. This means that the damage the wardstone creates, whilst not insignificant, is easily repaired through the simple passage of time. Especially on Ashenfall, where merely surviving to the next hour is an adventure.

Wardstones are great in a pinch, but one must be careful not to use too many, less the damage they deal to themselves becomes too great.
}}

|type = Consumable

}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Harralander

|mat2 = Redberries
|mat2qty = 1

|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Weak Healing Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Weak Healing Potion}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Weak Healing Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Alchemists have long strived to create potions to rid mankind of all their ills. Normally these potions are regeneratives, that boost the body’s ability to repair itself by drawing in the ambient anima and infusing it into the blood and tissue of the drinker. On the mainland this changes healing from a matter of days, to a matter of hours. On Ashenfall the results are far more pronounced.

The ambient anima of the region is so strong, that wounds heal in moments. Cuts vanish as if they never were, the wounds healing so quickly that even scars cannot form. The weaker potions burn themselves out before they can heal massive injuries, but the results are impressive none the less.
}}

Used to craft Barbed Arrows or to coat your equipped melee weapon with barbs, causing Bleed buildup for a short duration.

Sometimes you just really want to hurt something. It happens. We each have that little voice that tells us to make something bleed, just a little.

Well, blacksmiths thought to themselves “what if we listen to that voice?” and created these little doohickies. Barbs for your weapons, so that they may tear, rend and otherwise force your opponents to bleed with every strike.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Consumable/Weapon Poison
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Weapon Poison
|image = Weapon Poison.png
|type = Consumable
|description = A vial of Weapon Poison. 

Used in crafting Poison Arrows or to coat your equipped Melee Weapon with Poison for a short time.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Kwuarm
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Poison Ichor
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Clay Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Weapon Poison
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Weapon Poison}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Weapon Poison}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Used in crafting Poison Arrows or to coat your equipped Melee Weapon with Poison for a short time.

There are times in life where you really want someone to regret ever meeting you. It could be a long-held grudge, it could be on the battlefield, or it could be in a never-ending meeting that could so easily have been an email but has now persisted past your lunchbreak and you had so been looking forward to that sushi… ahem… sorry, where was I? Oh, yes, pain!

Poison is sometimes considered to be a coward’s weapon. Personally, I prefer to think of it as the smart weapon choice, after all, honour is only worth anything if you’re the winner of the battle. A corpse with honour still rots in the dirt. With a blade coated in poison, you can almost guarantee the death of your enemies, all it takes is a single nick and their fate is sealed.

An investment well worth making, if you ask me.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Consumable/Whetstone
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Whetstone
|image = Whetstone.png
|type = Consumable
|description = Use this to hone your equipped melee weapon.
|weight = 5.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Whetstone''' is a consumable item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Crafting Table
|mat1 = Granite
|mat1qty = 5
|mat2 = Stone
|mat2qty = 10
|output1 = Whetstone
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Whetstone}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The blade is not just a weapon in the wilds of Ashenfall, it’s a tool. Sometimes it’s the difference between seeing tomorrow and having a very bad today. 

Dwarves have always understood the importance of properly maintaining one’s equipment. They turned the care for their weapons into a ritual of almost religious significance. Blades would be cleaned, serviced and sharpened so that they might cut through deadlier dangers and challenge greater threats. 

The whetstone is a simple device, but one that served as a backbone of this ritual. A smith would learn to gently sharpen the blade to the thinnest edge possible, treating it almost like a competition. They would boast and brag with each other about the sharpness of their blades. 

A popular dwarven myth speaks of the Prime Whetstone, capable of sharpening a blade to the point that no armour or metal could withstand it. Sadly, or perhaps thankfully, no evidence exists to provide any truth to the myth. 
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Abraxus Ring
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Abraxus Ring
|image = Abraxus Ring.png
|type = Trinket
|weight = 1.0
}}
'''Abraxus Ring''' is a equipment other item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Trinket
|durability = 100
|carryweight = 1
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Jeweler's Bench
|skillxp = 100
|mat1 = Vault Shard
|mat1qty = 6
|mat2 = Clay Mould
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Silver Bar
|mat3qty = 2
|mat4 = Sapphire
|mat4qty = 2
|mat5 = Necrotic Bonemeal
|mat5qty = 8
|output1 = Abraxus Ring
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Abraxus Ring}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Little is known about the great necromancer, Abraxus, a fact he finds deeply frustrating. In his youth he sought secrets forbidden to others and he wandered the Whispering Swamps of Brynmoor in search of secrets that others dared not know. The swamp was ancient and haunted, so the legends went, and there was a magic to it that was different to any other part of Ashenfall. He explored vaults, caves and battled goblins in the mists, but never found the power that his youth demanded. Until one day, he found the smoke that promised him the power he so desperately craved. 

From there his history is clouded, obscured just as the mist obscured the swamps. He founded a small order of necromancers in Fellhollow and took advantage of the chaos when the Withering began. He sought to bind the souls of the dead into his service, gaining power just as Imaru gained hers from the consumption of the fallen. 

But Abraxus was no dragon, and so frustratingly mortal. A single hoplite spear is all it took to end his life, he would have been embarrassed if he felt it had mattered. As a spirit he lost a lot of things we take for granted, sensation, corporeality, and many of the emotions that everyone else enjoys. Yet, in exchange, he was given the most precious gift of all – time. Time to contemplate. Time to scheme. Time to think about what really matters, in the grand scheme of it all, and he has found his answer. 

His ring, imbued with his dark power, offers no value to him any more. So he bequeaths it to you. In time, perhaps, it may help you become a mage strong enough to earn his respect. Well, a small fraction of it, at least.  
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

Very popular in the hot summer months.

|mat5 = Vault Shard
|mat5qty = 6
|output1 = Amulet of Accuracy
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Amulet of Accuracy}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Wild Knights knew the dangers of missing their target. If an arrow did not slay its target quickly, then too soon would the quarry either escape, or, worse, gather friends. Wolf packs could easily tear a small group of Knights to pieces if not dealt with fast.

The Amulet of Accuracy made sure that hunts went smoothly. When a shot landed, it landed hard. Alpha wolves would be slain in a single well-placed shot, and sturdier threats might go down in a small flurry of arrows.

When Silverthorn Keep stood against the rising horde of zombies, these amulets were worn by the keep defenders who unleashed a torrent of arrows into the hungry dead.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Amulet of Defence
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item

|image = Amulet of Defence.png
|type = Trinket
|description = A mystical amulet that strengthens your defences.
|weight = 2.0
}}

|carryweight = 2
|specialeffect = A mystical amulet that strengthens your defences.
}}

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Amulet of Defence}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Not everyone can wear armour when faced with the hordes of shambling undead. Children and the frail, become targets with little to protect them from gnashing teeth and jaws. In these scenarios magic becomes incredibly important, and these amulets delayed the deaths of countless lives.

Not everyone was happy with such utilitarian approaches to survival, however. As the days drew on and resources grew scarce, empathy began to wane in the population of Emberwood. Amulets of defence were handed out to those determined to have the most need, but the criteria for that became a hot subject of contention.

It would be nice to imagine a world where everyone pulled together to defend against the undead, but human nature sadly shattered that dream. Still, while there would always be those who would attempt to steal the amulets from the needy, there were plenty more who would stand against them.

These amulets now carry an echo of those protectors, shielding its wearers from harm.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

{{Journal entry|
Emberwood had its fair share of spellcasters, though lacking the funding of the tower to the south. With more limited resources the mages of Emberwood found other methods to strengthen their art, and the Amulet of Magic was popular among all of them.

Druids worse these amulets while performing rituals around the standing stones, hoping that it might strengthen their bond with nature. The self-proclaimed warlocks, of the Children of Shadows, would sit in darkened basements wearing these glittering amulets as they intoned dark rites that had no effect. The Wild Knight mages would use them more pragmatically, wielding magic against all the threats that the villagers were unaware of.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Might makes right, is the cry of every wannabe despot. Bandos in particular was very fond of this statement. This amulet upholds that notion and does its best to help make sure you are the most correct person around.

It imbues your muscles with body-aligned anima, the magic strengthening the sinew and fortifying your bones. It adds greater force to your swing that will crush your enemies before you.

The goblin general, Snotknucke, wore this amulet and attempted to hunt down Velgar. He believed that, with its magic, he could fell the beast in a single swing of his club. Snotknuckle was stronger, deadlier and more powerful than ever, but this did very little against the rock that Velgar dropped on his head.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It is uncertain who first crafted the Anti-dragonfire shield. The dwarves claim the honour, as do several human blacksmiths. The goblins tried to take the credit, but their smithing techniques were not nearly refined enough to be believed. The aviansie might have been capable of such a feat, but if they did craft the shield, they never said. 

The aviansie that did visit Ashenfall only stayed in Brynmoor for a short time. They disappeared deeper into the continent, never to be heard from again. 

The Anti-dragonfire shield is a formidable defence, surpassing the strength of iron and capable of withstanding a dragon's lethal breath. Its previous owner, hopeful it could be a weapon against Velgar, was proven wrong. 

Only the most powerful individuals can hope to wield such an object. Yet, even so, it is said that the shield can be improved by combining it with the essence of a dragon, gaining some of the fury of the legendary beast.
}}

|mat3 = Demonic Oil
|mat3qty = 2
|output1 = Black Shield
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Black Shield}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
More and more of the afflicted began talking in the voice that Rasmodel recognised: the voice from his recurring dreams. They were a chorus, moved from the land of dreams to his waking nightmare. Doubt burned in him. Was this epidemic his fault? Was it here for him? 

He couldn't hide his relationship to the epidemic anymore. When he walked past the afflicted, they would move from incoherent to coherent. They would sit up and speak to him. The message was the same: "submit to the Dreaming Stone". There were variations on that theme: use the Dreaming Stone; spread its energy; offer your mind to it.

Lod Rasmodel was resolved. He would never submit. Perhaps therein was the trap. Perhaps it was shrewd psychology to make him destroy the stone. 

But destroy it he did. All it took was a single blow from his warhammer. A great and savage arc. As the crystal shattered and the shards punctured his skin, he felt peace. The voices were silenced. he knelt, bleeding and happy. 

And then the aftershocks came.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

It was Mittens that learned how to turn blurite into crossbow bolts. His pet, the blacksmith Gothren, had continued to rely on iron and bronze to get the job done, but Mittens had realised that there was great potential in this strange blue metal. He would ruin sand moulds, crafted by Gothren to forge blurite bars, making them appear as long thin strips. It took more ruined moulds than the cat had hoped for his owner to realise that blurite bolts would match their namesake crossbow.

Whether they were more effective, is of no value to a cat, appearance is, after all, everything.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Bronze Arrow
pages.text:

|name = Bronze Arrow

|type = Equipment Other
|description = Arrows with tips forged from bronze. 
|stacklimit = 999
}}

==Recipe==

}}

===Recipe tree===

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Bronze Arrow}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Bronze Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Bronze arrows offer greater armour piercing than their non-metallic counterparts. Copper and tin were relatively uncommon in Brynmoor, so using the resources on arrows seemed like a waste of money. One faction of bandits delighted in this premise; their leader, a man known as the Auburn Ocelot—for reasons he would never explain—took great amusement in their use. 

The Auburn Ocelot would laugh as his bandits hurled expensive arrows at the villagers, pilfering coins worth far less than the ammunition used to corral them. To him, it wasn’t about the money but the look of confused terror on their faces and the knowledge that no one could stop him. 

He was above it all. Better than everyone. 

It was a rat that killed him. Not even a giant one. Just a tiny creature carrying a virulent plague. Money could not save him, no matter how much he flaunted it. He was cremated, and his ashes buried in the middle of nowhere so that no one might risk catching the infection that cost his life. 

Now, he’s almost completely forgotten. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

===Recipe tree===

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The barbed bronze arrow was the ammunition of choice for the hunting party of Lord Gawurin, the regent of Ashenfall. He and his hunting party would head out into the woods alongside a small contingent of White Knight bodyguards. The lord would hunt deer, boars and other game there, but he wasn’t the most accurate shot. 

This arrow was designed for bloodletting, meaning he only needed any hit to begin the prey’s downfall. A single strike would start them bleeding, slowing them down and making them easier to hunt. The hunt outcome was all but inevitable. Poor sport, some would whisper, but the lord cared only for the end result. 

It’s perhaps fitting, then, that Lord Gawurin would be hunted with his own arrows on the night of Falling Embers. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Deadly arrows made deadlier with fire. Each arrow burns with the fury reserved for the most unpleasant parts of Infernus. They sear, they singe, and they melt the flesh, turning their victim into little more than ashes floating away on the wind.

In the days of the great war against the Garou, the priests of Saradomin encouraged the use of these weapons. They saw it not just as a means of eradicating the hated beastmen, but to purify the world from their taint. Lougrim was a false goddess, in their eyes, and her followers had to be cleansed from existence.

Modern scholars would doubtless be confused at their monotheistic mindset, considering that the literal god of war, Bandos, walked beside them into battle. The mental gymnastics employed by the Brynmoor priesthood, could have rivalled any athlete in the Gielinor Games.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Bronze Shield
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bronze Shield
|image = Bronze Shield.png
|type = Shield
|description = Block and parry attacks steadily with this basic, dependable bronze shield.
|weight = 10.0
}}

==Stats==

|block = 39

|mat2 = Leather
|mat2qty = 4
|output1 = Bronze Shield
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bronze Shield}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
While heavier than other materials, there’s little argument that a bronze shield is a superior defence to wood and leather materials. The metal turns aside almost any blow, protecting the wielder behind its mass. 

Even the Wild Knights, who usually shunned metal weapons, would don bronze shields into battle. Their philosophy was that a life is worth more than a principle. 

Before the peace treaty with their goddess, Lougrim, the garou would often get into conflict with travelling villagers. They saw the humans as prey, willfully intruding on their land and therefore consenting to be hunted. The humans, of course, saw things differently. 

Garou weapons were powerful and delivered by creatures of unnatural strength. Yet bronze would defend against these attacks, and it gave the garou pause, for they had yet to battle foe with such intriguing technology. Battles that would previously have been a slaughter became skirmishes as the garou tested their might against these new bronze defences. 

This comparative peace did not last long. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Compost Bucket
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Compost Bucket
|image = Compost Bucket.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = {HeldContainerDescription}

A wooden bucket used to carry a variety of Compost. Pour it's contents out over a Farming Plot to Fertilize your plants!
|weight = 5.5
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe

|skillxp = 50
|mat1 = Ash Logs
|mat1qty = 5
|output1 = Compost Bucket
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Compost Bucket}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The first bucket on Gielinor was created by Mrs Hyacinth, to help her around the farm. They had buckets back on the human homeworld of Teragard, of course, but she hadn’t brought anything with her from the village Guthix had recruited her from.

The problem was that Mrs Hyacinth had a cow, named the traditional Bessie, that produced an unreasonable amount of manure, and Mrs Hyacinth needed something to help with the clear up.

She used local twigs, at first, weaving together more of a basket than a bucket, and the results were not as watertight as she had hoped. In the end, she meticulously combined strips of wood, tied together with cord, and they were able to withstand even the worst of Bessie’s endeavours.

Now, you too can fill one of these marvelous contraptions with horrifically pungent compost. Spread the smelly stuff across your farming plots and marvel at the health and happiness of your leafy little friends. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

Strange magics have transformed the head of a tyrant, into a mystical adornment to this powerful shield. It is still strong, still durable and resists the fury of dragons, but now the shield seethes with Velgar’s wrath.

The magic of the shield is twisted into a bitter poison. One that yearns to strike and rage against any in its path. In battle, it’s possible to turn this poison on one’s enemies, afflicting them with a dragon’s hate.

Sometimes, in the dark, when the night is silent, it’s as if the shield whispers angry curses into the darkness. As if the last vestiges of Velgar’s fury are carried on the wind as hateful poison.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Earth Rune
User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Enchanted Blurite Bolt
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Enchanted Blurite Bolt
|image = Enchanted Blurite Bolt.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Blurite crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted Jade, that have a chance of dealing Earth magic damage and knocking your target down.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Enchanted Blurite Bolt''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 15
|mat1 = Blurite Bolt
|mat1qty = 33
|mat2 = Jade
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Astral Rune
|mat3qty = 10
|mat4 = Earth Rune
|mat4qty = 15
|output1 = Enchanted Blurite Bolt
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Enchanted Blurite Bolt}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
No one is entirely sure who thought to enchant blurite crossbow bolts in Brynmoor. The earliest references talk about Ignatius Wormwood, a failed apprentice wizard with too much time on his hands.

The story goes that he was challenged by one of the Knights to a duel, over some spat about a maiden or other. Evidently Ignatius may have been a failure in his robes, but worryingly successful out of them, if you get my gist.

Now, the knight was a large and imposing man -- referred to by his compatriots as the immovable boulder -- and angry as he was, he had no intention of killing Ignatius, so he gave him a simple challenge instead. If Ignatius could knock him down, he’d win the duel, otherwise the Knight would claim victory and see Ignatius punished for his mischiefs.

When Ignatius pulled the crossbow from his robes, the knight was concerned but knew that his armour and shield could stand against them. Yet, when the bolt impacted the knight’s pristine iron armour, he found himself hurled to the floor and the wind knocked out of him.

Gracious in defeat the knight let Ignatius go, only for the wizard to wander into the next person he had wronged.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Enchanted Bronze Bolt
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Enchanted Bronze Bolt
|image = Enchanted Bronze Bolt.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Bronze crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted opal, that have a chance to summon a lightning strike around your target, dealing Air magic damage to all nearby enemies.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Enchanted Bronze Bolt''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 15
|mat1 = Bronze Bolt
|mat1qty = 33
|mat2 = Opal
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Astral Rune
|mat3qty = 10
|mat4 = Air Rune
|mat4qty = 15
|output1 = Enchanted Bronze Bolt
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Enchanted Bronze Bolt}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Magic was never enough. Oh, sure, you could change bones into delicious peaches, you could summon water out of thin air, you could call down fire upon your foes and laugh as their flesh turns to ash (if you’re that way inclined). Yet, even so, it was never enough. There was always more death to deal, more damage to inflict, more pain to spread and the priests of Brynmoor wanted more of it.

They wanted to see the garou crushed. Wanted to see them begging as the last of their pups expired beneath Saradomin’s purifying flame. So, they commissioned the wizards and alchemists to find new and innovative ways to summon suffering.

Crossbow bolts proved to be an interesting revelation. By affixing gemstones to the tips of these weapons, not only could they inflict deadly wounds on their enemies, but they could also inflict debilitating pain so powerful that the opponent would be stunned, making the next attack much easier.

The priests were very happy with this discovery. The so called “heretics” within Brynmoor were less so.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Enchanted Iron Bolt
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Enchanted Iron Bolt
|image = Enchanted Iron Bolt.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Iron crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted Jade, that have a chance of dealing Water magic damage and slowing your target. 
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Enchanted Iron Bolt''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 15
|mat1 = Iron Bolt
|mat1qty = 33
|mat2 = Jade
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Astral Rune
|mat3qty = 10
|mat4 = Water Rune
|mat4qty = 15
|output1 = Enchanted Iron Bolt
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Enchanted Iron Bolt}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Stone remembers, wood forgets, iron holds a grudge. So goes the Imcamdo saying. A reference to how much stronger metal weapons were than wood and stone. Still, with magic, this adage can become painfully true.

The first enchanted iron bolts on Ashenfall were crafted by Lina Hesperian, one of the Amalgamated. She had hair that flowered like a waterfall down her spine. Her eyes were crystalline lakes, sparkling in the light, and her smile was as refreshing as any spring. She enchanted the bolts so that they might carry the gift of water, washing away infection for a clean and gentle kill. She made them for the hunters, so that they might claim their quarries with as little suffering as possible.

She met the end that far too many Amalgamated suffered. Alone, frightened, in the cold and the dark of an alley, her blood seeping from the knife wound in her chest. As she gave her last breath, she cursed the world for it’s cruelty, and the iron listened.

Now, each enchanted iron bolt taps into that grudge, inflicting a reminder of her suffering on any that feel the bolt’s sting.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Fallen Hoplite's Aspis}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The hoplites of Emberwood named themselves after Saradomin’s forces in the holy scripture. The legendary hoplites were warriors on the world of Hallow, who were trained from farmers and artisans, rather than military families or nobility, and the Emberwood hoplites came from similar stock.

They were best known for their fighting style using spears and shields, with the shield being the defining feature. These shields were designed to be worn for longer periods of time, resting against the shoulder for more comfortable carrying.

The hoplites may have been raised from peasant stock, but their camaraderie made them a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield.

As the withering took them, the hoplites retained the rote memory of their training and remained a deadly threat to all who faced them.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Fang Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Fang Arrow
|image = Fang Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Arrows with tips carved from animal fangs. 
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Fang Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Crafting Table
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 8
|mat1 = Small Animal Fang
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Ash Logs
|mat2qty = 2
|mat3 = Feathers
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Fang Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Fang Arrow}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Fang Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
For a short time, a necromancer arrived on Ashenfall. She had found her way here by boat, fleeing pirates who wanted revenge for her misdeeds. Her skills were limited; she could not raise undead legions, but she had a knack for controlling simple fangs and bones. 

She fled into the forests – pirates having found Ashenfall and hunting her down –  where she would unleash fang arrows against her foes in the hope of stopping their chase. It didn’t work. Broken, lost and without hope, she prepared herself for death. 

But death never came for her. 

Instead, a Wild Knight, who wore a mask made from the skull of a wolf, rescued her. He pushed back her assailants and brought her to safety. Their fellowship after that was unbreakable, though no bond of romance flowed between them. She made the arrows, he fired the bow, and together, they feasted on their victories and kept the woods safe from unwanted intrusion. 

Even now, there are signs of their friendship. Hidden across the forest, miniature carvings of a wolf skull and a bat can be found in places that meant something to them.
}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Fire Rune}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Guthix created runes in the first age, here on Ashenfall. He found a way of capturing the ambient wild anima, of the continent, in the floating rune essence around the region. He would then give these runes to his druids, teaching them the fundamentals of magic.

Fire runes contain enough magical fire to light a campfire. Battlemages prize them for the ability to lob that small fire at an enemy instead. Either way, they’re a very handy resource.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Magic lives in the dirt, the ore and the rocks. It crystallises inside gemstones, matrices of magical energy just waiting to be unlocked. In Brynmoor the priests of Saradomin kept the secrets of magical amulets within their purview. They would craft jewellery that would enhance the fighting instinct of their soldiers, and hand then out like candy.

As the faith in Saradomin rose, the faith in Guthix waned. Partially because of the more active and present proselytising of the Saraodminist priests, who where more vocal than the quiet druids, but also because the paladins and bronze knights seemed significantly stronger than the Guthix-aligned guard. These amulets being their secret weapon.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Inspiring Amulet of Magic
pages.text:

|type = Trinket

|weight = 2.0
}}

==Stats==
{{Infobox Trinket

|carryweight = 2
|specialeffect = This item loses durability over time when equipped.
}}

==Recipe==

|mat4 = Vault Shard
|mat4qty = 6
|output1 = Inspiring Amulet of Magic
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Inspiring Amulet of Magic}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It is perhaps not surprising that wizard’s made use of magical jewellery as early as possible. Indeed the first few pieces worn on Ashenfall were little more than decoration, and gaudy wizards would attempt to outdo one another with shinier and shinier pieces.

As part of their ever increasing “prank war” wizards began to create jewellery that was enchanted with effects designed to frustrate, or otherwise hinder, the wearer, believing that enchantments in jewellery were little more than silly trinkets. One mage created what she assumed was an auditory illusion, within an amulet, the intention being that when the wearer would try and cast a spell, the jewellery would play an annoying earworm tune, distracting the poor caster.

This did not happen. Something about the threads of magic, the structure of the crystal and the concealing spell that was intended to hide the prank, created something unique. Instead of making it harder to cast spell, the wearers found it easier. Understanding came faster, spells and incantations clearer than before.

What was intended to distract instead focused the wearer, allowing them to concentrate in ways they had never been able to before.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Wild Knights used many tools and tricks in their hunts. Looking for any edge they might have against the untamed wilds of Ashenfall. One jeweller, a water-Amalgamated by the name of Lochlan, created an amulet imbued with the communal spirit of the hunters. He would leave the gemstone, that would serve as its base, in the gathering hall and soon it became a tradition for each hunter to touch the gemstone if their hunt was successful.

Over the years this gemstone took on their pride, prowess and skill and that became a magical enchantment that lay within the heart of the stone. Crafting an amulet from the crystal, Lochlan found a way of sharing that enchantment with everyone who wore it.

Now, millennia later, the echoes of that enchantment linger within the crystals, just waiting for someone to remember them.  
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

For most, though, they simply find themselves in a trance like state – able to ignore the world around them and focus on their craft. Learning more and more about it with each moment that passes.

The dwarves know that the earth has a memory, gemstones especially so. What they don’t tell you, is that the earth holds a grudge. Each time a pick pierces the stone or smashes a piece of ore from its home, the earth remembers.

A skilled enchanter can use that grudge, that earthen memory, and turn it back against the grudge-filled earth. Traditionally this is done via jewellery, with dwarven goldsmiths making most of their living from miners purchasing these trinkets.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Ashenfall was the first place that Guthix created runes, but he never passed those secrets on to his followers. It wasn’t until the legendary human, V, discovered the secret, that it was taught to the general population.

Still, something about Ashenfall makes the process easier. No altars are needed, nor talisman’s required, yet runes can be crafted with an abundance that would make the mainland guild’s jealous.

This ring taps into that ease, infusing the soul with some of that ancient knowledge of the island. Be careful sleeping with this on, as you may dream the memories of runecrafters past.

Or of a dog made of cheese. No one knows why.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
If trees could move, they would kill us all. That’s the terrible truth that no lumberjack speaks of, instead they silently protect the rest of us from arboreal wrath. When a lumberjack was trained by the druids in Brynmoor, and taught the secrets of the murderous woods, they would be gifted a ring to help them in their battle against the vile trees.

These trinkets have helped lumberjacks defend us all from the cruelty of the woods, in a war that most don’t even believe is happening.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

===Recipe tree===

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Iron Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Iron arrows are sturdy and sharp, capable of piercing most armour. Brynmoor soldiers regularly employed them in the war against the garou. 

Village blacksmiths toiled for weeks at a time, creating countless arrows to be slung into battle. They poured literal blood, sweat and tears into the cooling metal, each arrow containing a shard of their hate for the beastmen. 

The arrows were effective, brutally so. The arrows forged by blacksmiths who had lost loved ones to the conflict seemed to hit harder still. Vengeance manifested in metal. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Iron Barbed Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Iron Barbed Arrow
|image = Iron Barbed Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Lacerating arrows with barbed tips forged from iron.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Iron Barbed Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 8
|mat1 = Iron Arrow
|mat1qty = 33
|mat2 = Weapon Barbs
|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Iron Barbed Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Iron Barbed Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The first of these weapons was not commissioned for war. Nor was it commissioned for hunting. Instead, this most deadly arrow was created for nothing more than vengeance.

Against a pigeon.

Lord Gawurin was the ruler of Brynmoor, as appointed by the priests of Saradomin. He wore expensive garments of the finest materials, adorned with glittering jewels and gold chain. So, he was most affronted when a single pigeon determined that such a garment was the perfect restroom choice.

The pigeon's audacity was unforgivable. Day after day, Lord Gawurin's garments were defiled by this feathered fiend. His anger grew, fuelling a desire for revenge that consumed him. He commissioned an arrow, a weapon of brutal design, with a single purpose- to make the pigeon suffer.

With a sinister smile, he summoned the most skilled hunter, a woman who had brought down countless foes, and ordered her to end the pigeon's reign of terror.

She stood at the top of the castle, her bow steady, her aim true. The tension in the air was palpable as she prepared to unleash the arrow of vengeance upon the pigeon. The world seemed to hold its breath in anticipation.

She missed. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
One sad part of human nature is that they will constantly strive to craft deadlier and deadlier weapons. Iron crossbow bolts were harder than their bronze counterpart, capable of penetrating the toughest garou armour.

Obrimex, of the garou, once tried to replicate these weapons. He crafted little sharpened tubes of iron that he felt would match their power. Alas, the garou hadn’t yet crafted crossbow technology, and throwing these little metal darts largely proved to be irritating rather than dangerous.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Iron Fire Arrow
|image = Iron Fire Arrow.png

|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Iron Fire Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Iron Fire Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Rasthin was the master of fire. It was the gift she had stolen from the “soul of the world” as she called it. She could use the inner flame to inspire confidence or rouse people into a great fervour. The fire could reveal secrets, forcing truth from those who stood before her. But, more practical than all of that was one simple truth, that fire could burn.

There were many a dragon that tried to battle Rasthin directly, believing their dragonfire could destroy her. Their bones litter Ashenfall. It was only Queen Kuldra herself who could challenge the dragonkin, and theirs was a battle that left scars in the lands for centuries.

These arrows borrow a piece of Rasthin’s power. Able to inspire only by the fear they kindle in the wielder’s enemies. Yet, kindle they do, for those who face these flames will surely feel echoes of the fury wrought by the dragonkin lord of fire.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Iron Shield
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Iron Shield
|image = Iron Shield.png
|type = Shield
|description = A reliable, durable shield crafted from iron, perfect for tanking big hits.
|weight = 10.0
}}
'''Iron Shield''' is a equipment other item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Armour
|power = 4
|durability = 1300
|block = 46
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Anvil
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 250

|mat2 = Hard Leather
|mat2qty = 4
|mat3 = Vault Shard
|mat3qty = 4
|output1 = Iron Shield
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Iron Shield}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
When Glass Knife was caught, she had no concerns about safety. She was well armed as a blacksmith, better than most hunters who tracked her down. She wielded a dagger in one hand and an iron shield in another, and she snarled defiance at those who tried to condemn her for her crimes. 

She was, after all, protecting humanity. Purging impurity. Ridding them all of the Amalgamated stain on their bloodlines. In her mind she was doing the whole village a service. A worrying number of villagers agreed with her. 

As the wife of one of the elders, her sentence was small. An act of community service, doing what was already her job. She wasn’t imprisoned. She wasn’t executed. She wasn’t publicly condemned for her crime. She laughed off over a dozen murders with a cruel, smug-filled smile. 

While she carried an iron shield with her, and it weathered the reprisal of many an Amalgamated enraged at the lack of justice, her real shield was the status she held. Wife of an elder, blacksmith of the town, proof that when an Amalgamated was a victim, justice had its limits. 
}}

A leather shield consists of a thick piece of leather stretched across a wooden frame. Wooden shields are bulky and heavy, making them more unwieldy. In contrast, a leather shield’s relative lightness makes it much easier to move into position. 

Leather shields were popular with the villagers of Bramblemead since the materials were comparatively easy to gather. Young warriors often wielded them as they accompanied druids into the woods, where the druids gathered the herbs for their medicines and rituals. 

The Children of Shadows also enjoyed leather shields, which they decorated with Zamorakian symbols, hoping to intimidate anyone who saw them.
}}

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Mithril Barbed Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Demons dealt with wounds and ailments on their own. There was no such thing as a demon surgeon or sick bay. If wounds were fatal then the demon would die; if wounds were survivable, then survive they did. 

This was an observation of Summoner Flament, who passed it on to Alric, who idly mentioned it to Lord Rasmodel in one of their weekly meetings. As with most information, Lord Rasmodel examined it for opportunities.

If demons sought no help from their wounds - no remedies, no medical assistance - then his knights would carry weapons that took advantage of that fact. Arrows were poisoned, since no demon carried antipoison. And arrows were barbed, as demons would pull them roughly from their hides, rather than remove them carefully. 

It was a small advantage, but Lord Rasmodel took it. As he would often say, "stack opportunities, one on top of the other, and soon you will have an impregnable tower". 
}}

pages.text:

|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Mithril Fire Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Mithril Fire Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Fogger's Gala was one of the few dates that a Black Knight could look forward to. An annual tradition, it would take place on the mistiest day of the year. As soon as low clouds started rolling in, word spread among the knights that it was Gala day.

It was an informal event on the battlements of the keep. There were quivers that represented each of the night's events, and the knights would name their arrows and place them into the quivers of those they wanted to participate in. Arrows were drawn randomly to determine the order. 

Sergeant Blister was on hand with betting. He brought his blackboard, which he seemed to have on-hand when needed, and a week's wages were placed on friends and adversaries. 

The events all revolved around bows and crossbows. The fog made it almost impossible to see anything, so the Gala was a test of both memory and accuracy. One event required the archer to hit hay bales with fire arrows. The hay bales were invisible, so they only had location names to go by - the hut by the waterfall, Skrexis's bed - which meant firing Hail Mary's in the vague direction of their quarry, hoping that it would ignite in the night.

Other events included Pin the Arrow on the Goblin (as you can probably imagine, a noisy goblin ran about in the fog), Farthest Arrow and Alric's Curtains.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

There's at least a little science behind that. The more tempered mithril is, the more it reflects. Doric isn't being a narcissist here: he knows what he is doing. 
}}

The Children of Shadows loved using these. The very notion of bone and poison combined as a weapon; it touched some deep dark part of themselves and filled them with a malicious sort of glee.

Alas such weapons proved relatively useless in any secret campaigns against the Saradominists of Bramblemead. Certainly, they could assassinate a baker if they needed to, but against a knight it would just ping harmlessly off their armour.

Still, they made for good talking points at the secretive meetings, making enjoyably sinister table-decorations that paired well with the wine and cheeses.
}}

|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Poisoned Iron Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Lougrim abhorred the use of poison on arrows, she felt it cheapened the hunt. The mages could wield it in their spells, but a hunter should produce smooth, quick kills, creating as little suffering as possible.

Most garou, in turn, would at first refuse to use poisons on their arrows. They followed her teachings, and they hunted swiftly and cleanly. After Lougrim’s fall it became clear just how foolish it was to seek such a merciful path, their new High Queen had made it clear that they should use all tools at their disposal.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Poisoned Mithril Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Poisoned Mithril Arrow
|image = Poisoned Mithril Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Arrows with tips forged from mithril, coated in Weapon Poison.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Poisoned Mithril Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 8
|mat1 = Mithril Arrows
|mat1qty = 33
|mat2 = Weapon Poison
|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Poisoned Mithril Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Poisoned Mithril Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Rasmodels were notorious for their use of poison. When they lived to the south, in Temple Woods and Bramblemead, it was often said that you should cover your cup, mouth and ears around a Rasmodel. One of the family was finally caught red-handed (or green-handed, considering the poison used), and the rumour moved to certainty. They were banished to Dowdun. 

The reputation followed them, and mostly became a gift rather than curse. No-one in Dowdun Reach wanted to cross the Rasmodels for fear of waking up in spasms. 

We say that it was 'mostly' a gift, because of an event between a young Lord Rasmodel and his cousin. Lord Rasmodel claimed that he could shoot an apple from the top of his cousin's head, and promptly did so. He handed one of the resulting apple-halves to his cousin who ate it, and promptly keeled over and died.

It turned out that the arrow used to the cleave the apple was poisoned. Lord Rasmodel claimed that he had been using his father's hunting arrows, and one of them must have been tainted. It seemed entirely plausible.

But there was still doubt. And that doubt grew into a reputation: a reputation that outmatched his own family's. Even at the age of eight, the inhabitants of Dowdun Reach gave Lord Rasmodel a wide berth.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

Harold has a job for you and won’t let something as trivial as your expired mortality get in the way. This piece of enchanted jewellery is primed to go off when you’re about to land on Death’s list, whisking you away to safety before the scythe needs to be swung.

There are many sorcerers that have studied these rings for years, going slowly mad as they try to uncover the secrets of immortality. If they could just learn the methods by which this ring operates, then they could extend their lives indefinitely.

Only one wizard ever actually learned this secret, but he forgot the method by which this ring works, for it teleports you at the moment of your death. As the ring does not stop the aging process, this unfortunate mage now spends his eternity in perpetual teleportation – hurled through the abyss, never to take form again.
}}

It didn’t prevent the bruises, or fix the broken bones, but their assailants attacks would end much more quickly when they discovered they weren’t as untouchable as they thought.

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Salve Amulet
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Salve Amulet
|image = Salve Amulet.png
|type = Trinket
|description = A powerful amulet made from a crystal that has absorbed the magical blessings of the River Salve.
|weight = 2.0
}}
'''Salve Amulet''' is a equipment other item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Trinket
|durability = 2500
|carryweight = 2
|specialeffect = A powerful amulet made from a crystal that has absorbed the magical blessings of the River Salve.
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Jeweler's Bench
|mat1 = Clay Mould
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Gold Bar
|mat2qty = 2
|mat3 = Silver Bar
|mat3qty = 2
|mat4 = Salve Crystal
|mat4qty = 1
|mat5 = Vault Shard
|mat5qty = 6
|output1 = Salve Amulet
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Salve Amulet}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The name is a bit of a misnomer, since this amulet has very little to do with the river Salve. Instead its name refers to the crystal that traditionally sits at the heart. This crystal was first brought to the region by Saradominist priests, some time in the third age. They had harvested it from a cave near the river Salve and found ways to cultivate on Ashenfall’s soil, by utilising the natural magic infusing everything.

Sanctified by faith and magic, this amulet was deadly to the undead, and the dragonkin Lacrussa was fascinated with the properties. She stole the recipe for its creation and hid it within her vault, long before the withering curse spread over Fellhollow. Instead she had delved too deeply in her own experiments and discovered something buried within the earth that would twist and reanimate the dead.

This amulet allowed her to hold the forces back and keep them from overrunning her lab. That was, until, she met her end at Kuldra’s fangs.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Steel Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Steel Arrow
|image = Steel Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Arrows with tips forged from Steel.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Steel Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 30
|mat1 = Steel Bar
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Ash Logs
|mat2qty = 2
|mat3 = Feathers
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Steel Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Arrow}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Steel Arrow}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Steel Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Traditional wisdom says that to kill a zombie, you must destroy the brain. This is disappointingly untrue in Fellhollow, the animating force of Imaru’s hunger needs no thought to drive it forward.

No, a far better plan is to destroy the bones and muscles with sharp metal-tipped spikes launched at the creatures with as much force as you can manage. Steel arrows perform this task very nicely.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

|output1qty = 33
}}

==Journal==

Sometimes it’s not enough to just kill your enemy. There are times when you really want them to suffer as well. Steel arrows are nasty enough, but covering them in deadly barbs that will rend the flesh and empty the liquid lifeforce of a person onto the forest floor? That’s just overkill.
}}

|image = Steel Bolt.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Crossbow Bolts made from Steel.

}}

==Recipe==

|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Bolt}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Steel Bolt}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Sharpened bolts of steel projected at great speed is a formula for your target to have a very bad day.

Although it was cheaper and quicker to forgo the coal and craft iron bolts, the relentless nature of the undead hordes required a more practical approach. Steel was stronger than iron, and was more likely to put the ravenous undead back in the dirt where they belonged, making it very popular in the last few years of Fellhollow’s resistance.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Steel Shield
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Steel Shield
|image = Steel Shield.png
|type = Shield
|description = A reliable, durable shield crafted from Steel, perfect for tanking big hits.
|weight = 10.0
}}
'''Steel Shield''' is a equipment other item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Armour
|power = 5
|durability = 1300
|block = 52
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Anvil
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 250
|mat1 = Steel Bar
|mat1qty = 10
|mat2 = Draconic Leather
|mat2qty = 4
|mat3 = Vault Shard
|mat3qty = 6
|output1 = Steel Shield
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Shield}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
There are few things as good at shielding you from harm, as a great slab of metal between you and your foe. The steel shield is more durable than its iron kin, capable of resisting greater and more deadly blows.

It was popular among Fellhollow locals, who would play a game that indicated an intelligence shortage among the population. The game consisted of one player being equipped with a crossbow and other players would hide around the area carrying steel shields. The crossbow wielder would then hunt the other players and try and fire a bolt into their shields, marking them as out of the game.

Unsurprisingly the injuries caused by this game were large and common place. Even the death of some of the players wouldn’t cause the activity to be banned. “It’s tradition”, “it’s just good fun”, “hardly anyone gets hurt”, these were the arguments made even as parents grieved the loss of their child from the game.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Steel Watering Can
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Steel Watering Can
|image = Steel Watering Can.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = {HeldContainerDescription}

A Steel watering can. Can be filled at any water source. Used for watering crops in Farming Plots.
|weight = 5.5
}}
'''Steel Watering Can''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Anvil
|skill = Farming
|skillxp = 50
|mat1 = Steel Bar
|mat1qty = 6
|mat2 = Padded Cloth
|mat2qty = 2
|output1 = Steel Watering Can
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Watering Can}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
With the discovery of steel, most people found new and wonderful ways to bring death. They crafted sharper swords, stronger shields, axes that could slice through a foe like a hot knife through butter. Yet, some people saw this new material, as a chance to bring life.

Steel watering cans were sturdier and more durable. Able to bring much needed moisture to dehydrated gardens than before.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Stone Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Stone Arrow
|image = Stone Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = Arrows with tips of splintered stone.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Stone Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 5
|mat1 = Stone
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Ash Logs
|mat2qty = 2
|mat3 = Feathers
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Stone Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Stone Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The stone arrow's weight and durability make it a more popular choice among hunters. The hardness of the tip makes it better at piercing tough animal hides or less durable forms of armour. In the second age, druids considered it a form of cheating, citing that it required a human's ingenuity to create rather than being a gift of nature. 

They seldom complained when the evening meal was served, celebrating the success of the hunt alongside everyone else. 

Even with somewhere as remote as Ashenfall, some humans turned to banditry, and they were very fond of stone arrows. Cheap and easy to produce, they help to kill and threaten anyone who makes the mistake of travelling through bandit-controlled woodland. 

One such bandit, the self-described "bandit king, Otho", would choose only "interesting" stones to turn into arrows. Glittering pebbles that swished through the air. He was nicknamed the "Magpie King" because of his love for shiny pebbles. A moniker he was very unhappy with.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Wooden Arrow
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Wooden Arrow
|image = Wooden Arrow.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = A very basic arrow.
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Wooden Arrow''' is a equipment other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Ash Logs
|mat1qty = 2
|mat2 = Feathers
|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Wooden Arrow
|output1qty = 33
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Wooden Arrow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Druids dislike violence but understand its place. The natural world is full of blood, murder and death. The strong consume the weak. Predator against prey. This they understand, though they seldom like it. When they must resort to violence, they choose the simple approach. Man’s strength comes not from their muscles but from their creativity and ingenuity. Just as a wolf has claws, a hunter has a bow. 

When a bow must be drawn, the druids prefer arrows made of simple wood. Gathered twigs and branches, whittled down to sharp points. They see it as the forest giving them the blessing to go on the hunt. Traditionally, feathers are gathered from around the forest floor, taken when dropped or from a bird’s moulting. This keeps the number of arrows low, forcing a hunter to be accurate and precise, lest they run out of chances. 

Thus, death is swift at the hands of a bow, and balance isn’t damaged. This the druids preached to the villagers. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Wooden Shield
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Wooden Shield
|image = Wooden Shield.png
|type = Shield
|description = Basic protection for those seeking to more efficiently block and parry.
|weight = 10.0
}}
'''Wooden Shield''' is a equipment other item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Armour
|power = 1
|durability = 500
|block = 26
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Crafting Table
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 25
|mat1 = Ash Logs
|mat1qty = 10
|output1 = Wooden Shield
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Wooden Shield}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The children of Bramblemead loved nothing more than to craft themselves wooden shields and swords and then challenge one another to single combat. They would play the roles of brave heroes against dastardly villains, whacking one another with their wooden toys. It gave the local healers constant work. 

Yey, a wooden shield is no toy and has saved the lives of countless warriors. As a beast’s claws slice against you, having a hunk of wood between you and the creature is much appreciated. Wood splinters, but flesh bleeds, and blood is less easily replaced than timber. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/EquipmentOther/Wooden Watering Can
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Wooden Watering Can
|image = Wooden Watering Can.png
|type = Equipment Other
|description = {HeldContainerDescription}

A Watering Can made from Ash Wood. Can be filled at any water source. Used for watering crops in Farming Plots.
|weight = 4.5

}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Rain is unreliable. It is far too whimsical in when and where it falls. Relying on its good nature is a sure-fire way to court drought, famine and ruin. Because of this, the druids created rainfall you could hold, handheld containers of water that could be summoned when and where it was needed.

Fill one of these up from any water source and you can use it to keep your farming plots nice and moist.
}}

pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bread
|image = Bread.png
|type = Food
|description = Crusty brown outside, pillowy white inside.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Bread''' is a food item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Kiln
|skill = Cooking
|mat1 = Bag of Flour
|mat1qty = 1
|output1 = Bread
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bread}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Bread}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Simple wheat, water, yeast and salt, combined together and incinerated within an oven. A staple across many cultures and species across Gielinor and beyond. The Icyene of Hallow enjoy a flatbread that they can wrap food in, for a meal on the wind. The aviansie of Abbinah, when the limited wheat is available, prefer a thick and sturdy bread, so that each bite lasts longer. Elves enjoy a sourdough, priding themselves on how long they can keep the starter alive.

For the people of Ashenfall, much like their cousins in the mainland, a simple loaf is more than enough.
}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Cauterised Undead Steak}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Despite the appearance of their former owners, and the deeply unpleasant smell emanating from it, undead meat is not rotten. It, like the nature of its former owners, is preserved in a state between life and death, as if it’s on the cusp of souring, but never quite getting there.

This unique nature gives it some very interesting properties, but sadly taste isn’t one of them.
}}

}}

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Fried Cabbage}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Fried Cabbage}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Brassica Prime – the god of cabbages – gifted the world with this delicious vegetable, to lead the faithful on the noble path of nutrition. In his infinite, leafy, wisdom, he taught the early settlers about how one might fry the leaves, so that they would be even more delicious.

Now these fried cabbages offer those who consume them, the strength and stamina needed to brave the hardships of Ashenfall.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Food/Fried Egg
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Fried Egg
|image = Fried Egg.png
|type = Food
|description = Eggs, sunny side up, a nutritious snack.
|weight = 0.4
|stacklimit = 99
}}

==Recipe==
