|facility = Campfire
|skill = Cooking
|mat1 = Egg
|mat1qty = 1
|output1 = Fried Egg
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Fried Egg}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Fried Egg}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Slimy, greasy and everything you need. There are few things as surprisingly satisfying as a fried egg sandwich. If only you had some bread!

Contrary to popular humour the aviansie have no issue with people eating eggs around them. Indeed many of them have raptor like tastes and will happily consume the eggs of livestock birds. Boiled almhas (a domesticated bat-like creature) eggs were popular snacks on Abbinah, yet those eggs could not be fried as they would combust dramatically when exposed to a naked flame.

Fortunately chicken eggs are far less flammable.
}}

|name = Maple Syrup

}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Maple Syrup}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
We are yet to find something that maple syrup does not elevate. Pancakes and bacon are the obvious ones, but try it out on things that are more left-field. Rat meat? Elevated. Terrorbird meat? Elevated. Undead eel? Eelevated. 

So, thank you, humble maple tree. You are a true wonder of the culinary and apothecary world. It won't stop us woodcutting you, but know that each cut comes with a maple syrup-sized dollop of guilt.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

Try not to think too hard about the nature of transmutation as you consume this fruit. It's sweet, juicy and the pseudo-necromantic origins only make it a little disturbing. Oh, sure, it tastes nice and it goes surprisingly well in a crumble, but it's a little disturbing when you get that unexpected crunch. 

Best not to think about it. Ignorance is bliss. 
}}

}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
A taste of autumn reduced to slurry and warmed over the fire. In Fellhollow, pumpkin soup was a popular dish when the temperature started to drop. Locals would gather together and share enormous cauldrons full of soup, as a way to build bonds with their community. It is said that once the villagers created an enormous cauldron, so that Imaru and Xikotal could join in the tradition, though for the great dragons it was barely an appetiser.
}}

{{Journal entry|
Get a nose peg for this one. The aroma that wafts from this culinary creation is almost unholy in its sensation. It reaches down from your nostrils into your throat, then it winds its way into your mouth and twists your tastebuds in ways that must surely be illegal.

Still, if you can fight through the stench, then this eggy abomination is surprisingly tasty and helps fortify your body against toxic elements.

The “if” is doing a lot of work in that statement though.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Food/Roast Dinner
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Roast Dinner
|image = Roast Dinner.png
|type = Food
|description = It's not even Sunday.
|weight = 2.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Roast Dinner''' is a food item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Range Stone
|skill = Cooking
|mat1 = Flank Steak
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Baked Potato
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Dried Redberries
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Roast Dinner
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Roast Dinner}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Imagine it. You gather around the table, the warm smell of a freshly cooked roast wafts across the room. Your mother places a bowl of roast potatoes, so divinely crisp that you wonder if they’re even real. Your sibling places a tray of freshly cooked vegetables, a kaleidoscope of colourful foods waiting to be consumed. Your father, doggedly sticking to traditional gender roles as if the past is an anchor weighing him down, slices into the roast serving each of you juicy cuts of meat that make your mouth water.

And what are you doing in all of this? Hrmm? Nothing, that’s what! Pull your life together Stanley!

If your name is not Stanley, you can ignore that above message.

Here on Ashenfall you do not have those smiling faces to reflect upon, only the memories elucidated by the roast meat before you. As you tuck in, you’ll recall those moments of happier times, before you got stranded here with nothing.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Sausages are proof that ignorance is bliss. When you bite into the juicy banger, you are best served not to think about its contents. Nor about the origins of the skin that contains the meat so well.

Although simple, and lacking in much needed herbs and spices, these sausages manage to curb your hunger well enough to help you avoid thinking too much about what they’re made from.

Ignore the squeak… and the whiskers.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Sweet Veg Ball
|image = Sweet Veg Ball.png
|type = Food
|description = A marvelous amalgamation of sweet and savoury. 
|weight = 0.4
|stacklimit = 99
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe

|skill = Cooking

|mat3 = Dried Redberries
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Sweet Veg Ball
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Sweet Veg Ball}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Popular among gnomes, this sticky ball of fruits and vegetables is a combination of savoury and sweet that almost feels like it shouldn’t work. Yet it does.

There is a crispness to the outer shell, savoury and salty, which gives way to a delicious fruity sweetness beneath.

Gnomish ambassadors would create large numbers of these sweet treats, piled high on silver platters, to delight visiting dignitaries at lavish receptions. Diplomats would often joke about being spoiled by such treats. Which is ironic really, as these spoil very quickly, so enjoy them now while you can.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Antifire Potion
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Antifire Potion
|image = Antifire Potion.png
|type = Other
|description = Removes Burning stacks and grants future protection to Burning. 
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Antifire Potion''' is a other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Irit
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Blue Dragon Scale Dust
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Glass Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Antifire Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Antifire Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
While the antifire potion is a miraculous thing - one of the most useful items on Ashenfall - it does have a flaw. It can be hard to know when its effect wears off. Knowing that you are protected, or not protected, while standing in front of a dragon is something of a problem.

One solution is to bring someone with you. Get them to count while you fight a dragon. Then they can give you a happy thumbs-up while protected and a sad thumbs-down when you're not. 

Of course, they probably need antifire if they're going to assume that role. Which means a third-party to give a thumbs-up or thumbs-down for that second person. Yeah, we see the flaw in the plan.
}}

Congratulations on killing enough Black Knights to form a bar from their resulting scraps. Don't think about the children you have orphaned, the dreams that you have impaled on the sharp end of a greatsword. Don't think about the concentrated misery you hold in your hands. 

It is rather shiny, though, is it not? Black Metal, particularly if you have the draconic sort (more chromatic, less matte), is mesmerising.

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Black Metal Scraps
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Black Metal Scraps
|image = Black Metal Scraps.png
|type = Other
|description = Dropped from Black Knights. Can be reforged into black metal at a blast furnace.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Black Metal Scraps''' is a other item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Black Metal Scraps}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Fun fact: black metal is actually steel that has gone through a form of vulcanisation. Demonic or draconic sulphur are used in the process, to slightly different effect. Demonic vulcanisation gives the metal a matte finish, while draconic is more chromatic. 

What you have here is some scraps of black metal. Could be demonic, could be draconic. They're likely off-cuts from the smithing or vulcanisation process, or it's entirely possible that they were bashed off a Black Knight's armour when you boffed them. Regardless, waste-not, want-not. Now you have the material required for some new gear.
}}

}}

==Products==

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Cursed essence is a magical agent often used in tanning. It is also something of a curiosity, one that a simple tanner can't readily explain. It goes further than simply binding itself to material; it latches onto it, almost parasitically. 

Demonologists have a theory that, although cursed essence drops from avernic demons of Dowdun Reach, it is actually cthonic in nature. It would explain the tendril-like tenacity.
}}

{{Crafting Tree|Cursed Leather}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Cursed Leather}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
You may wonder where the 'cursed' moniker comes from. There is no need to worry: you're not cursed by wearing it, and you're not going to be visited by the three dragons of past, present and future in the night for making it. It has a wildly different etymology. 

The 'cursed' is a tannery thing, you see. Cursed leather is notoriously hard to work and shape. Only the foremost tanners can manipulate it. But even they have a hard time, often exclaiming "cursed leather!" at the top of their lungs.
}}

}}

{{Journal entry|
Combine corrupt bonemeal and vile ashes and the result is demonic oil. We should be specific about what kind of oil this is: please do no pour it into a frying pan or use it on a squeaky hinge. This is potent oil, as the 'demonic' prefix will attest. It is used for crafting, and solely for crafting.

Now you've got us wondering what would happen if you did actually add it to a frying pan or a squeaky hinge. Obviously, please don't do it. But if you do happen to do it, through an accident or momentary lack of wisdom, could you let us know what happens? 
}}

Still, this potion makes you acutely (haha, see what I did there?) aware of angles. You can measure a plank of wood with just a few well placed glares. The inner architect in you revels at the new possibilities before you, and you find yourself planning much further ahead.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Focused Farming Potion

|type = Other
|description = Increases XP gain for the Farming skill. Made with irit and undead bones.

|stacklimit = 50
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Irit
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Undead Bone
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Glass Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Focused Farming Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Focused Farming Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
A common misconception is that a farming potion is poured onto your crops, rather than into your mouth. While it might sound sensible - it's the crops that are growing, not you - it fundamentally misunderstands what the farming potion does.

The farming potion increases the experience you receive when you farm. It achieves this by making you sensitive to the wants and needs of your plants. You become intensely aware of them. You will know when even a single aphid lands on a leaf; when the last drop has been siphoned from the soil; when a potato wants to hear you sing. You are attuned to your crops, to the point that you might even find kinship with them. If you begin sleeping in your cabbage patch, you should consider lowering your consumption.
}}

==Journal==

While the weaker variant of antipoison is sufficient for everyday usage, there are some situations that require a more potent resistance. The toxic mists in Ghornfell, over the swamps, are more powerful than a weaker resistance will protect against. These lesser potions strengthen the immune system and keep the toxic miasma from the drinker’s blood. Imperfect, still, but a much greater chance of survival.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Lesser Attack Potion
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Lesser Attack Potion
|image = Lesser Attack Potion.png
|type = Other
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Snapdragon
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Ground Granite
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Clay Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Lesser Attack Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Lesser Attack Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
No one is entirely sure who invented this potion, but those without the knowledge were invariably the losers in warfare. In ancient times armies would be given doses of this potion before they were sent off to battle. It made them deadlier, faster and vastly increased their chances of victory.

The Children of Shadows used to try brew large quantities of this potion every time they went off on a mission, so that they would have the edge were they ever caught. Many a guard could attest to the power of this potion, but fortunately it didn’t make up for a general lack of melee skill.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Lesser Magic Potion
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Lesser Magic Potion
|image = Lesser Magic Potion.png
|type = Other
|weight = 1.0

}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Snapdragon
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Wild Anima
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Clay Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Lesser Magic Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Lesser Magic Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Although humans cannot use magic without runes, they can become quite proficient conduits for power. A wizard and a farmer, when possessing the same runes, do not produce the same results, the former far outshines the former. 

This made some farmers pretty upset. For years they had ranted to their cows and sheep about how wizards were just snooty uppity types with shiny rocks. Yet, faced with this revelation, there was no denying that a farmer and a wizard simply weren’t equals in disguise. 

One farmer wanted to level the playing field and brought in a local witch who was had a grudge against the snooty men in blue robes. 

Between the two of them, they found a way to improve a person’s connection to the magic within them via a magical potion. Those who drank it cast spells more efficiently and with greater skill than they ever could before. 

The farmer wanted to use this new advantage to put the wizards in their place, raze down the tower and claim magic a tool of the people. Unfortunately, the witch saw a different future, one that sparkled with glittering gold coins. 

She died happily, wealthier than she had ever dreamed, surrounded by adoring young suiters who wanted a piece of her inheritance. The farmer died after being trampled to death by cows that were tired of his grumbling. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

You could also find the odd chicken wing from the Second Age. The Children of Shadows loved a shake and bake.

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Mystic Cloth
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Mystic Cloth
|image = Mystic Cloth.png
|type = Other
|description = A magical textile, woven from mythic thread.
|weight = 0.6
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Mystic Cloth''' is a other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Loom
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Mystic Thread
|mat1qty = 3
|output1 = Mystic Cloth
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Mystic Cloth}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Mystic Cloth}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It is almost imperceptible, but mystic cloth hums. It's the magical resonance: the elemental threads pushing against each other as their charge shifts. That oscillation also means that there is a lightly massaging effect on the wearer. It's entirely possible to feel calm from the vibrations as you stride into battle.

You might also be mistaken for thinking that mystic cloth is blue. On a microscopic level, it's actually a multitude of colours, combining to form the illusion of blue. As a magician once told me, it's the same effect as a blue sky: it is the result of all the colours in the world combining to make wondrous azure.  
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Plant Cure
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Plant Cure
|image = Plant Cure.png
|type = Other
|description = Used to cure diseased plants and bring them back to a healthy state.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 20
}}
'''Plant Cure''' is a other item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Range Stone
|skill = Farming
|skillxp = 10
|mat1 = Weeds
|mat1qty = 2
|mat2 = Animal Bone
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Clean Water
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Plant Cure
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Plant Cure}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Fashioned from clean water, weeds and animal bones, this mixture leeches out the vitality from those ingredients and transfers it to the sick and dying plants in your care. Cooked over a range, this concoction was first thought to be the domain of druids and herblore masters, but in truth it was a cook that put the pieces together. 

Old mother Potterington was much beloved by the people of Bramblemead. On weekends, when people allowed themselves brief breaks form their toils, she would whip up a great batch of something delicious and simmering, and neighbouring farmers would drop by for a meal. 

This kindness extended not only to people, but to her garden and farming patches too. She whipped up a broth so infused with nutrients, that Brassica Prime himself is said to have blessed it. When fed to plants, their withering and wilting leaves would stiffen and return to vibrancy, life and vitality theirs once again.

Now you can brew such a concoction yourself, so that your plants might always live their healthiest days.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Other/Quarrymaster Potion
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Quarrymaster Potion
|image = Quarrymaster Potion.png
|type = Other
|description = Applies the Quarrymaster perk, which helps to decrease stamina lost from Mining. 
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|mat1 = Irit
|mat1qty = 1

|mat3 = Glass Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Quarrymaster Potion
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Quarrymaster Potion}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
As with many physical activities, you can over-exert yourself when mining. Lactic acid builds up in the muscles, sweat clouds the vision, and an accidental pickaxe swing can lodge in the head. A mining potion addresses all of these shortcomings. Lactic acid and sweat forms slowly. The likelihood of accidents is reduced.

What it can't do is improve the quality or amount of ore. Pour it in your mouth or pour it on the deposit: it won't matter. No mining potion is going to help you there.  
}}

They’re also surprisingly good for a hangover.

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
This potion is sweet, yet strong. The ingredients have stewed within the mixture for some time and allowed it to increase in potency. While it’s perfectly fine to drink it directly from the bottle, you find yourself wishing you had a nice mug to use instead.

Still, this potion makes you acutely (haha, see what I did there?) aware of angles. You can measure a plank of wood with just a few well placed glares. The inner architect in you revels at the new possibilities before you, and you find yourself planning much further ahead.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/QuestItem/Challenge Horn
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Challenge Horn
|image = Challenge Horn.png
|type = Quest Item
|description = An ornate dragonkin horn, traditionally used to challenge someone to a duel of honour. Bring it to the dragonkin altar to challenge Velgar.
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Challenge Horn''' is a quest item item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Anvil
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 15
|mat1 = Dragon Tooth
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Bloodwood Sap
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Bronze Bar
|mat3qty = 5
|output1 = Challenge Horn
|output1qty = 0
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Challenge Horn}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Cathan’s challenge horn design is a collection of materials hastily thrown together. A dragon’s tooth, carved with dragonkin sigils as well as metal wires used to connect them together –  like a spiderweb lattice, glistening with traces of magical power.

When placed in the dragonkin altar, the horn acts as a focus point for the magical power stored within the tower, sending an invisible beacon off into the air, in a perfect draconic pitch.

Velgar has been summoned many times before. By heroes, kings, warriors and gods, and yet still the tower stands.

Be ready, when you send out the call.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/QuestItem/Crystal Seed
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Crystal Seed
|image = Crystal Seed.png
|type = Quest Item
|description = A resonating crystal, waiting for the right song.
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Crystal Seed''' is a quest item item.

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The crystal tree is a magical thing, a native to the elven world Tarddiad and brought to Gielinor when the elves migrated. Unlike most seeds it is nourished not by soil, nor sunlight, but by music. Elf song reaches into the crystal, creating a harmonious resonance that causes the crystal to thrive.

As well as growing trees, this seed can also be sung into specific shapes and forms, becoming a tool or weapon of great power.

This seed is a little different to those found in Tirannwn, infused both with the magic of Ashenfall, but also the essence of cherished memories.
}}

}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/QuestItem/Purified King's Token
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Purified King's Token
|image = Purified King's Token.png
|type = Quest Item
|description = A ritually cleansed token containing a piece of another world. 
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Purified King's Token''' is a quest item item.

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
These tokens are a unique creation, crafted hundreds of years ago by high priests of Lougrim. They are passed down from monarch to monarch, a sign of their overwhelming power and strength. This token has been blessed as part of an ancient Garou ritual, symbolically wiping away Gielinor's "corruption". Although it looks no different to before it was purified, it feels strangely lighter and is a little cold to the touch. 

Inside each token rests a single pebble taken from Antumnos, the Garou homeworld, and if you focus your senses you're sure you can feel the faintest pulse from within. 
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/QuestItem/Velgar's Head
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Velgar's Head
|image = Velgar's Head.png
|type = Quest Item
|description = Proof of your triumph over the Dragon General. It's dripping blood all over, you should probably get rid of it...
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Velgar's Head''' is a quest item item.

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Velgar's Head}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
General Velgar was one of Queen Kuldra’s most feared disciples. Infused with the power of the earth itself, his poison corrupted entire regions and slaughtered countless foes.

The fall of Blue Flame Keep, Bramblemead village, and the Saradominist presence on Ashenfall, was orchestrated by Velgar’s hand. His army incinerated villagers, priests and soldiers alike. His forces tore down castles and houses with equal relish.

For thousands of years, Velgar has been a thorn in everyone’s side, until one hero took him down.

Bring this to the Wise OId Man, rumours are that he can make something useful from it.
}}

pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Blightwood
|image = Blightwood.png
|type = Resource
|description = Logs cut from ancient trees or roots.
|weight = 1.5
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Blightwood''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Blightwood}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
There is something about the swamps of Ashenfall that encourage strange trees to grow. The result is blightwood, durable, powerful and somehow capable of channeling more potent magic than other trees.

The wild knights joked that blightwood houses the souls of the victims of the swamp, and that they are imbued with their regrets and hatred.

It’s probably not true. Probably.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Bloodwood Sap
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bloodwood Sap
|image = Bloodwood Sap.png
|type = Resource
|description = Harvested from bloodwood trees, found in the toxic swamp in Ghornfell.
|weight = 2.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Bloodwood Sap''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Bloodwood Sap}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Bloodwood trees are a genuinely horrible creation. Their very existence a wound in the decency of the world, because this tree grows only in places steeped in violence and death.

The biggest bloodwood trees tower over battlefields. Sinewy spiderlike branches cast shade over the homes of serial killers, and you should never trust anyone that can grow one in their garden.

Leech-like roots soak up the suffering, sorrow and cruelty that has seeped into the soil, and then converts that misery into a sticky red sap of raw emotion. This sap can be used to amplify feelings, to convey anger, fear or hatred, across a much greater range.

The dragonkin Lacrussa was very fond of this medium, and used it’s power to shackle dragons to her will.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Blue Dragon Scale
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Blue Dragon Scale Dust
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Blue Dragon Scale Dust
|image = Blue Dragon Scale Dust.png
|type = Resource
|description = Ground from blue dragon scales. Integral to making antifire potions.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Blue Dragon Scale Dust''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Grinder
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Blue Dragon Scale
|mat1qty = 1
|output1 = Blue Dragon Scale Dust
|output1qty = 2
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Blue Dragon Scale Dust}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Blue Dragon Scale Dust}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Blue dragon dust is one of those things where you wonder who invented it, and what curious sequence of events led to it happening. Some unknown person had the wherewithal to strip a dragon of dragon scales, crush those scales in a pestle and mortar, then apply the resulting dust to a potion. Not only that, they downed said potion and stood in the path of a dragon. Does that not seem inexorably mad to you? 

But thank you, inexorably mad person, because now we have this potent ingredient which can protect a person from dragonfire. You have been forgotten in time, but your blue dusty legacy lives on.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Blurite Bar
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Blurite Crossbow Limbs
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Blurite Crossbow Limbs
|image = Blurite Crossbow Limbs.png
|type = Resource
|description = A pair of blurite crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Blurite Crossbow.
|weight = 4.5
}}
'''Blurite Crossbow Limbs''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Forge
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Blurite Bar
|mat1qty = 2
|output1 = Blurite Crossbow Limbs
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Blurite Crossbow Limbs}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Blurite Crossbow Limbs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Not all cats are terrified of crossbows, though most of the Bramblemead population quickly learned to fear them. Some cats could see their merit but were painfully aware of their human pet’s limited imagination.

Mittens was the lifelong owner of Gothren Actillian. A blacksmith’s cat, he had learned how to keep the forge fires perfectly hot by playfully jumping on the bellows periodically – something his pet seemed to enjoy. He knew the right length of time a sword should be cooled for and would make sure his human stuck to that timing by offering useful alarms made of glorious clattering things.

But blurite? That was a new one. Mittens could see there was little value to the metal, beyond some weird human aesthetic that he did not understand, yet there was some potential to the construction. With carefully timed meows, and some creative knocking over of reagents, Mittens was able to engineer a crossbow of superior design, worthy of his cherished pet.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Blurite Ore
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Blurite Ore
|image = Blurite Ore.png
|type = Resource
|description = A chunk of rare and unrefined Blurite.
|weight = 2.3
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Blurite Ore''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Blurite Ore}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Sometimes colours just want to manifest themselves. There is the sweetness of orange. The cowardice of yellow, and the peaceful serenity of a summery green. Blue, tired with only being linked to sorrow and sadness, sought a physical existence. One that could be shaped into new and wonderous things, and so blurite was born.

A metal that is so very, very, blue.

It’s in the name. After all, it’s blue, right?
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Bone Crossbow Limbs
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bone Crossbow Limbs
|image = Bone Crossbow Limbs.png
|type = Resource
|description = Crossbow limbs made from powerful bone. Used in crafting the Dorgeshuun Crossbow.
|weight = 4.5
}}
'''Bone Crossbow Limbs''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Forge
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Undead Bone
|mat1qty = 2
|output1 = Bone Crossbow Limbs
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bone Crossbow Limbs}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Bone Crossbow Limbs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Following certain incidents with cats and crossbow limbs, a superstition began to build in Bramblemead about the danger of crossbows and felines. It was considered bad luck to house a cat in the same home as a crossbow, with reasoning ranging from the safety of the cat, to the safety of the human population should the cats rise up and develop opposable thumbs.

Whilst harmless at first, when the need for crossbows grew due to the conflict with the garou, cats found themselves with fewer and fewer homes willing to house them. Over the years this led to domestic cats being a rare sight in Bramblemead.

It also created a deep-seated resentment in the cats, and they plotted their revenge. Most would tell you that cats had nothing to do with the destruction of Bramblemead and the Brynmoor settlements, yet if you ask a cat, they will simply purr with a sinister grin on their face.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Bronze Bar
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Bronze Crossbow Limbs
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bronze Crossbow Limbs
|image = Bronze Crossbow Limbs.png
|type = Resource
|description = A pair of bronze crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Bronze Crossbow.
|weight = 4.5
}}
'''Bronze Crossbow Limbs''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Forge
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Bronze Bar
|mat1qty = 2
|output1 = Bronze Crossbow Limbs
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bronze Crossbow Limbs}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Bronze Crossbow Limbs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The complicated mechanisms of the crossbow sparked a small technological revolution in Bramblemead. Such devices had once been the domain of the dwarves, who guarded their advanced techniques to maintain their smithing dominion. However, soon human smiths found themselves creating these complex trigger mechanisms.

There were casualties, certainly. Some mechanisms tightened strings too greatly, snapping back with a force that could cause serious harm.

One notable example involved the pet belonging to the village priest, a curious feline with absolutely no sense of self-preservation. The cat entered the workshop, as she so often did, and took it upon herself to knock everything she could to the floor. Unfortunately, in doing so, she triggered the release mechanism being worked on and quickly learned that cats are not meant to fly.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Cabbage
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Cabbage
|image = Cabbage.png
|type = Resource
|description = Yuck I don't like cabbage.
|weight = 0.2
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Cabbage''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Cabbage}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Few vegetables are as worthy of such reverence as the humble cabbage. Delicious. Easily portable and possessing of a leaf more noble than any other. This, at least, the followers of the god Brassica Prime profess.

No one is entirely sure how there is a god of cabbages. Fewer still are sure why there is a god of cabbages, yet the existence of such a being cannot be denied.

Unlike other deities, who guard the focus of their faith with great jealousy, Brassica Prime delights in nothing more than seeing people enjoying the delicious, nutritious, wonders of the humble cabbage.

Best enjoyed fried or mixed into a stew. Cabbage wine is a failed experiment that should never be spoken of again.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

This journal entry was sponsored by the tree haters of Lumbridge, if you’re interested in joining their focus group, please speak to Tim near Bob’s Axes.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Dire Wolf Leather

{{Infobox Item
|name = Dire Wolf Leather
|image = Dire Wolf Leather.png
|type = Resource
|description = The tanned hide of a savage beast.
|weight = 0.6
|stacklimit = 50

|output1 = Dire Wolf Leather
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Dire Wolf Leather}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Dire Wolf Leather}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The tanned hide of a powerful dire wolf. It's sturdy, thick, ready for enchantment and more resilient than normal leather. 

A legend speaks of a warrior that wore the tanned hide of a dire wolf. He wore it proudly as he walked the streets and used it to strike fear into the hearts of bandits and criminals alike. After all, a warrior that could best such a beast, was no one to be trifled with. 

The story goes that one day he met another warrior, proud, skillful and handsome, and the two of them became the best of friends. They would fight together, spar together and rescued helpless villagers as an unstoppable pair. Until the night of the full moon, where the truth was revealed. Where once his new friend had stood, there was now a dread wolf of midnight-black fur and eyes of burning coal. They fought again, but gone was the jovial sparring of friends. Now they fought as man and beast, tooth and claw versus blade and shield, until all that was left of the two of them was this story. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

|type = Resource

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Tanned hide infused with a dragon’s vigor, draconic leather was a gamechanging discovery to the people of Fellhollow. It was as flexible as normal leather, but more resilient than even hardened leather, and it could be fashioned into a variety of shapes useful in a variety of armoured styles.

For a short while, the fashionistas of Emberwood village would use this leather to create garments that evoked the majesty of their draconic protectors, without bearing the scales that might cause undue offence.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Fire Oil
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Fire Oil
|image = Fire Oil.png
|type = Resource
|description = Flammable and volatile, handle with care!

Used to craft Fire arrows.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Fire Oil''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Naphtha

|mat2 = Coarse Thread
|mat2qty = 1
|output1 = Fire Oil
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Fire Oil}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Fire Oil}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Used to craft Fire arrows.

Some people just like to watch the world burn… and you can too, with these new handy fire oils! That’s right, just lob one of these beauties at your enemies and watch them ignite. Enjoy their screams as their skin boils away and breathe in the delightful aroma of a fresh barbecue!

Fire oil, like snake oil, was once the creation of a travelling merchant to Ashenfall. She had journeyed to Brynmoor alongside some of the Saradominist settlers, and with her she brought pungent unguents, elucidating elixirs, marvellous medicines, and weapons of localised destruction.

When her wares summoned the call for refunds, she would dazzle the onlookers with a sudden fiery display and then disappear into the alleyways until she had been forgotten. She would then return in one of a staggering array of disguises, until the tragic day when her wagon hit a pothole during a quick getaway and her many, many, bottles of fire oils decided to sing the song of their people.

Fortunately, even though she and everything she owned, tragically found itself reduced to smouldering atoms, her recipe lived on and found more conventional uses.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Granite
|image = Granite.png

|description = A dense stone used for construction and crafting. Very commonly found underground, in mines and dungeons.
|weight = 2.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Granite''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Granite}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
This stone is stubbornly resistant to magic. Whereas other stones absorb the nascent anima, granite refuses. It exists on its own terms, not the whims of magic. As such it is highly prized in construction projects, as it resists most magic and what enchantments do affect it, last much longer.

Legend speaks of a weapon forged from this stone. A great hammer, said to be able to crush almost any armour.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Green Dragon Hide
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Green Dragonhide Leather
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Green Dragonhide Leather
|image = Green Dragonhide Leather.png
|type = Resource
|description = Made by tanning green dragonhide. A key crafting component for armour.
|weight = 0.6
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Green Dragonhide Leather''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Green Dragonhide Leather}}

==Products==

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Is it ingenuity of hubris to refine the mighty scales of a dragon down into this thick and sturdy leather?

To the Wild Knights it was the natural order. Hides became leather, which became a resilient second skin they would wear whilst riding into battle. Favoured by archers and scouts due to the nimble flexibility of the scales, whilst still acting as a resilient bulwark against sword, arrow and claw alike.

To the people of Emberwood village, however, such sights were akin to blasphemy. Whilst the green dragons showed little mercy, the villagers loved and adored their majestic draconic protectors, Imaru and Xikotal, and they thought that wearing the skin of their kind might potentially cause offence.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Hollow Bark
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Hollow Bark
|image = Hollow Bark.png
|type = Resource
|description = Found lying around Fellhollow, around long-dead trees. Used in the crafting.
|weight = 2.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Hollow Bark''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Hollow Bark}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It is perhaps strange that bark could be referred to as hollow. It’s generally extracted in strips, or patches, but nothing that could act as a container. Indeed there would need to be an entire tree without a centre to make it hollow, and that’s exactly the case here.

When the withering began it was souls that suffered first, torn away and dragged into the underworld to be consumed by the cursed dragon. Yet, as time drew on, Imaru’s hunger did not diminish, only grow, and so the world itself began to suffer.

Things taste worse in Fellhollow. Dreams are vapid and empty, if you have them at all. A campire feels colder, even if it warms you just as well as before. Because the substance, the soul of that thing if you will, is being consumed.

Hollow bark comes from a tree that has been hollowed out from within. Its insides less than they should be, weaker and wrong. As awful as this is, it makes the bark develop in strange ways desirable by skilled mages with a poor sense of fashion.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Infernal Fragment
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Irit
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Irit
|image = Irit.png
|type = Resource
|description = Havested from Thorny Irit Roots, used in potion making at the brewing cauldron.
|weight = 0.2
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Irit''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Irit}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It is unsurprising that irit grows in Fellhollow, considering it has such a close association with the dead. Whereas normal irit grows around graveyards and near the temples of death deities, the irit in Fellhollow grows everywhere.

Yet, unlike its leafier version, the irit here is twisted and sharp. It is formed of thorny roots that writhe out of the ground and must be cut down to access the herb beneath. After all, the dead in Fellhollow refuse to stay buried without a fight, why wouldn’t the irit react the same?
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Iron Bar
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Iron Crossbow Limbs
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Iron Crossbow Limbs
|image = Iron Crossbow Limbs.png
|type = Resource
|description = A pair of iron crossbow limbs. Used in crafting an Iron Crossbow.
|weight = 4.5
}}
'''Iron Crossbow Limbs''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Forge
|skill = Artisan

|output1 = Iron Crossbow Limbs
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Iron Crossbow Limbs}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Iron Crossbow Limbs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Following certain incidents with cats and crossbow limbs, a superstition began to build in Bramblemead about the danger of crossbows and felines. It was considered bad luck to house a cat in the same home as a crossbow, with reasoning ranging from the safety of the cat, to the safety of the human population should the cats rise up and develop opposable thumbs.

Whilst harmless at first, when the need for crossbows grew due to the conflict with the garou, cats found themselves with fewer and fewer homes willing to house them. Over the years this led to domestic cats being a rare sight in Bramblemead.

It also created a deep-seated resentment in the cats, and they plotted their revenge. Most would tell you that cats had nothing to do with the destruction of Bramblemead and the Brynmoor settlements, yet if you ask a cat, they will simply purr with a sinister grin on their face.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

|weight = 3.0

In the eastern lands of Wushanko, jade is highly prized. Some islands have elaborate rituals about its usage. There are rumours of rare jades with magical properties, each a different colour of the rainbow.
}}

Crushed down, so that it’s sap and oils can be easily accessed, this herb is a requirement in some of the deadliest and most dangerous poisonous mixtures.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Maple is a versatile tree. It's a formidable material, making some of the finest fishing rods, staves and bows. Its sap is also a glorious thing, capable of being made into nature's nectar: maple syrup.

Its leaf is also smooth and large enough that it can be folded over numerous times, which makes it useful if you're caught short in the forest. Which, we should add, is not, and will not be, a consideration for you. It is extremely unlikely that a 'being caught short', hunger-like bar will be added for you to maintain. Realism can be pushed too far.
}}

pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Maple Sap
|image = Maple Sap.png
|type = Resource
|description = Harvested from maple trees. An ingredient that can be cooked on a campfire.
|weight = 2.0
|stacklimit = 999
}}
'''Maple Sap''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Maple Sap}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It's like the maple tree wants to be cut down. Not only does it produce hardy, versatile wood for tools and weaponry, it bleeds sap that is only a single step away from being maple syrup. If you want proof of the gods' existence in RuneScape, you need only look at maple trees. 

The maple tree gets its revenge in subtle ways. Its sap is inordinately sticky and too much syrup will give you a paunch. They're small victories, but the maple tree takes them. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Marrentill
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Mithril Bar
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Mithril Bar
|image = Mithril Bar.png
|type = Resource
|description = A versatile metal used for crafting weapons and armour.
|weight = 5.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Mithril Bar''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = AdvancedSmelter
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Mithril Salvage Pile
|mat1qty = 9
|mat2 = Coal
|mat2qty = 3
|output1 = Mithril Bar
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Mithril Bar}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Mithril Bar}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
In the Third Age on Ashenfall, mithril was incredibly desirable. It was only available in small quantities then, as its scarcity - perhaps inevitably - made it the fashion of the time. High-priests would incorporate it into their rosaries. Community leaders would wear it as brooches. It was rare enough that people would comment when they saw it. "Ooh, Rosie's doing well for herself". That sort of thing. 

The idea of a mithril bar would have been mind-boggling to anyone in the Third Age. No matter their alignment, they'd have stabbed you in the belly and run away cackling with it. But here you are with an entire ingot. Take a moment and consider yourself a Third Age king. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Mithril Ore
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Mithril Ore
|image = Mithril Ore.png
|type = Resource
|description = Mined from Mithril deposits. It can be smelted into bars at a furnace.
|weight = 4.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Mithril Ore''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Mithril Ore}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Mithril means much to the dwarves. It was first discovered by a dwarf called Tolkun in the First Age, and its seams have been uncovered in much of Gielinor. The dwarves call it 'The Dwarven Welcome' thanks to its shine. Find the ore when mining and it lights up to greet you: a dwarven welcome. 

A 'Dwarven Welcome' is also what dwarves call a headbutt while wearing a mithril helmet. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Monstrous Fang
User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Mystic Fibres
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Mystic Fibres
|image = Mystic Fibres.png
|type = Resource
|description = Dropped from Zamorak magi. Can be spun at a spinning wheel into mystic thread.
|weight = 0.6
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Mystic Fibres''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Mystic Fibres}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The origin of mystic fibres, thread and cloth are wreathed in mystery. There's little to no mention in any written histories, which - on its own - seems unlikely. It is not a subtle material. There should be log after log of merchants trading it, or actively avoiding trading it. 

It leads me to believe that the mystic material is an elaborate troll. It's a plant - and I don't mean that in the floral definition. I think someone has introduced it as a joke, and I have a list of potential culprits. Brassica Prime: you are number one on that list. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Mystic Thread
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Mystic Thread
|image = Mystic Thread.png
|type = Resource
|description = Spun from mystic fibre. Magic courses through every thread.
|weight = 0.6
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Mystic Thread''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Spinning Wheel
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Mystic Fibres
|mat1qty = 4
|output1 = Mystic Thread
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Mystic Thread}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Mystic Thread}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
There are many steps to making mystic robes - fibres to thread, thread to cloth, cloth to robes - but you would be wise not to skip any of them. Habberdash the Impatient, a magician who lived in Temple Woods, attempted to make his robes solely from thread. He would wrap himself, round and round, with spool after spool of mystic thread, and then attempt battle.

You can imagine how it went. He got snagged on a goblin's helmet and quickly unwound, mid-battle. We're not sure who would have been more shocked: Habberdash, who promptly got stabbed in the gut, or the goblin, who was confronted with a wizard in his undergarments.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.text:

|name = Naphtha
|image = Naphtha.png
|type = Resource
|description = A very flammable liquid.
|weight = 0.5
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Naphtha''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Swamp Tar
|mat1qty = 1
|mat2 = Charcoal
|mat2qty = 1
|mat3 = Clay Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Naphtha
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Naphtha}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Naphtha}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Naphtha}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Distilled from Swamp Tar and Charcoal.

Once known as “thick water”, naptha is liquid fire in waiting. It takes little to bring this substance to ignition, even too bright a sunny day can bring the raging flame from within.

As such, this material is widely sought by anyone with a strong desire to set things aflame. Pyromaniacs, alchemists and dragon cultists alike yearn for this liquid.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Necrotic Bonemeal
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Necrotic Bonemeal
|image = Necrotic Bonemeal.png
|type = Resource
|description = Made from ground up undead bone. A useful crafting ingredient.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Necrotic Bonemeal''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Grinder
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Undead Bone
|mat1qty = 3
|output1 = Necrotic Bonemeal
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Necrotic Bonemeal}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Necrotic Bonemeal}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The walking dead are strange things, animated by hunger, need, rage and a touch of magic. In Ashenfall it is the hunger of Imaru that drives them, as her great maw draws in the souls of both the living and the dead, the afflicted try to cling to life with everything that they have.

They fail, but the drive remains. Fear, desperation, and simple need keeps the body moving, if not alive. This changes the body, that hunger and rot seeping into the very bones and changing them to be something other than they were.

When ground down, these bones become a powder containing hints of that animating magic. A necromantic resonance with a wide range of uses.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Potato
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Potato
|image = Potato.png
|type = Resource
|description = Definitely not a carrot.
|weight = 0.2
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Potato''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Potato}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Baked, fried, mashed, there are few vegetables as versatile as the potato. Buried beneath the soil, these tubers absorb the nutrients around them, ready to be ripped from the soil and consumed.

Some mages noticed that you could pass certain magical currents through them, and one even attempted to enchant one and fashion it into blades. Suffice to say their plan was not successful, but it did create the very first chip, so was largely considered to be a good thing.
}}

pages.text:

|name = Pumpkin
|image = Pumpkin.png
|type = Resource
|description = More treat than trick. Can be cooked by itself or used as an ingredient in cooking meals.
|weight = 0.2
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Pumpkin''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Pumpkin}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
They seem strange without faces, don’t they?

Heralds of the spookiest of seasons, the humble pumpkin exists in two worlds, the world of the living and the world of the dead. When carved it acts as a ward against dangerous spirits and when mixed with spices and added to warm drinks, it acts as a lure for ex-girlfriends, distracting them as it gives you time to escape.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Sacred Oil
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Sacred Oil
|image = Sacred Oil.png
|type = Resource
|description = Made from combining necrotic bonemeal and ectoplasm. Used as a crafting component.
|weight = 0.5
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Sacred Oil''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Brewing Cauldron
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Ectoplasm
|mat1qty = 3
|mat2 = Necrotic Bonemeal
|mat2qty = 3
|mat3 = Clay Vessel
|mat3qty = 1
|output1 = Sacred Oil
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Sacred Oil}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Sacred Oil}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Although the recipe is significantly different to the oil created in Morytania, this oil serves a similar purpose. The priests of Emberwood created this oil to assist in funerary rites, the strange scent of the oil would help the dead pass on when cremated and, crucially, in the time of the Withering, the bodies would be reduced to ash, unable to get up and cause further trouble.

Originally this oil used pressed olives, and the influence of the gods to create an oil that would burn a body sufficiently to avoid the dead rising. As the Withering increased and civilisation began to fall into ruin, the priests found alternative methods to create a sufficiently strong flame.
}}

}}
'''Sapphire''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Sapphire}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Your eyes are like sapphires, sparkling in the night
Every syllable you utter creates pure delight.
A day in your company is infinitely bright
Will you be my valentine tonight?

A poem by a lovestruck Bramblemead youth. It was not successful.

Aside from their use in terrible teenage poetry, sapphires are a vital component in jewellry crafting. They can be found hidden among the metal ores around Ghornfell, or hidden in the various chests left behind by the old kingdom.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Steel Bar
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Steel Bar
|image = Steel Bar.png
|type = Resource
|description = Made by smelting coal and iron ore together. A primary ingredient for steel weapons, armour and tools.
|weight = 5.0
|stacklimit = 50
}}
'''Steel Bar''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Furnace
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Steel Salvage Pile
|mat1qty = 9
|mat2 = Coal
|mat2qty = 3
|output1 = Steel Bar
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Bar}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Steel Bar}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
If iron had been a marvel, steel was a revelation. Sturdier structures. More durable armour. Weapons sharper and stronger than ever seen before. If the people of Brynmoor had known about steel, then perhaps the garou threat would have been over almost as soon as it began.

For the people of Emberwood, it led to superior farming tools, stronger buildings and better hunting equipment. After all, what need did people have for weapons and armour with a dragon protecting them?
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Steel Crossbow Limbs
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Steel Crossbow Limbs
|image = Steel Crossbow Limbs.png
|type = Resource
|description = A pair of Steel crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Steel Crossbow.
|weight = 4.5
}}
'''Steel Crossbow Limbs''' is a resource item.

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Smithing Forge
|skill = Artisan
|mat1 = Steel Bar
|mat1qty = 2
|output1 = Steel Crossbow Limbs
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Steel Crossbow Limbs}}

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Steel Crossbow Limbs}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Steel crossbow limbs are sturdier than their counterparts, with a greater durability that allows them to create stronger and more dangerous crossbows.

Indeed, this durability seems to have put a nominal end to the unfortunate curse between cats and crossbow limb related catastrophes.

As a result, in Fellhollow, cats still had a place in the home, even after they had been systematically outcast from Bramblemead. It was said that Xikotal, in particular, enjoyed the gentle purring noises that they made and would surround his lair with hundreds of the creatures so that he might be lulled into a gentle sleep.
}}

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Undead Bone
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Undead Bone
|image = Undead Bone.png
|type = Resource
|description = Dropped from the restless dead. Use as a crafting material, or process at a grindstone.
|weight = 1.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Undead Bone''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Undead Bone}}

==Item sources==
{{Drop sources list|Undead Bone}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
You may think an undead bone is no different from a normal bone, and in most cases that’s true. The animating forces of necromancy normally just puppet the bones, allowing them to be possessed by a spirit or controlled by some external force.

The undead of Fellhollow are different. They animated by the last remnants of their humanity, tiny bits of soul clinging on in tattered forms. They move because they need to and they shudder because they fear what happens when they stop.

As such, the bones of the Fellhollow dead have strange properties that a skilled craftsman can refine and find uses for.

Morbid, yes, but inventive.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

User:RSDWArchive/Resource/Vault Core
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Vault Core
|image = Vault Core.png
|type = Resource
|description = A dragonkin energy source, found only within dragonkin vaults. 
|weight = 50.0
|stacklimit = 25
}}
'''Vault Core''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Vault Core}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
A heart of alchemically modified rune essence. Layers of complex enchantments, and anima veins run through this core. It allows it to absorb ambient anima from its surroundings, which in term power the various functions of the vault guardian servitors.

This marvel of dragonkin science had revolutionary potential. The ability to create power sources that thrived off of the ambient anima of the region. Ashenfall, with it’s surfeit of magical power, was the perfect place for such a technology and the dragonkin would apply it to all of their arcane machinery.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

|weight = 3.0
|stacklimit = 99
}}
'''Vile Ashes''' is a resource item.

==Products==
{{Uses material list|Vile Ashes}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Demons do not leave bones behind on death. That would require a skeleton, a prospect that would make a demon queasy. Instead, demons are magical entities, composed of essence and energies. On death, they disperse, leaving behind an ashy composite.

Don't be mistaken in thinking that it's similar to the ash left after a fire. Dab your tongue in vile ash and you'll soon understand where the 'vile' comes from. 
}}

Fallen from too many tall buildings, only to be punished for your misstep by the cruel and malicious ground? Well, now is your chance to get your revenge. The smug soil doesn’t think you can touch it, but you can prove it wrong by stabbing it through it’s smug topsoil smirk with a nice steel spade.

Oh, sure, it’s great for gardening, but really this is a tool for sweet, sweet revenge.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Tool/Undead Spade
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Undead Spade
|image = Undead Spade.png
|type = Tool
|description = A strangely spooky spade.

Used to clear farming plots.
|weight = 5.0
}}
'''Undead Spade''' is a tool item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Tool
|power = 5
|durability = 450
}}

==Recipe==

|skill = Farming
|skillxp = 50

|mat4 = Draconic Leather
|mat4qty = 2
|output1 = Undead Spade
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Undead Spade}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
In Fellhollow the dead refuse to stay buried. All save one.

Jenniva Milgrim was a fan of her rest, she liked nothing more than to ensconce herself within her blankets and disappear into a nice dream. Mornings became lost to her and soon evenings joined her campaign and she would spend most of her existence in the myriad worlds of her subconscious.

Then, one cruel day, she died. A victim of one of the first waves of the risen dead. She died, then awoke again as one of the rampaging horde.

Except she did not rampage like the others. She did not cross the lands in search of souls to claim, or flesh to consume. Instead she sought comfort once again in blankets and cushions, but found that sleep was denied her, as dreams are the province of the living.

There was precious little capability for thought in what remained of her brain, but a singular idea took hold. Since one buried the dead, perhaps that was where the dead could dream? Six feet under the soil, in blissful sleep.

So it was, that Jenniva became the first of the dead to bury themselves, using this very tool to do so.

It is said, that if you can find her grave, buried deep in the woods, you can still hear her snoring.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The abyssal demon is a foul beast. A single one can massacre a village before the warning bell can even sound. Crafting a weapon from their remains symbolizes triumph against adversity or perhaps just hubris in its purest form. 

Infused with the hate of the demon, each whip crack generates unbearable pain. Victims find themselves overcome with suffering as the agony seeps into the wounds the whips leave behind. 

It is said that the whip even feeds on that misery, growing stronger with each strike, each life taken. The hate imbues the weapon with a desire to inflict more pain, and it grows stronger each time such a wish is granted. Or, so the legends say. 

Only one Wild Knight has ever chosen to wield the whip in vengeance against the demon for a fallen sibling. Her name is dust in the wind of history, but there are still creatures that hide in the shadows, afraid of the crack of her whip.   
}}

The expedition to Ashenfall included skilled adventurers. If fate had been kinder, they could have gone down in legend as mighty heroes, rather than a slow, quiet, death where their names might be forgotten.

This bow was a design brought to Ashenfall by the hunter, Finn, a simple longbow crafted from easily obtained materials. Finn was a scout, and a ranger, who enjoyed getting into all the nooks and crannies when he would wander White Wolf mountain in search of treasure. He joined the expedition in hope of discovering new wonders, and new stories to share with the people back home.

Instead he found a dragon, and the end to his story.
}}

pages.title: User:RSDWArchive/Weapon/Ash Battlestaff
pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Ash Battlestaff
|image = Ash Battlestaff.png
|type = Magic Weapon
|description = A magical weapon carved from ash wood.
|weight = 15.0
}}
'''Ash Battlestaff''' is a weapon item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Weapon

| durability = 900
| attackstyle = Staff
| damagemulti = 1.9
}}

|mat3 = Coarse Thread
|mat3qty = 2
|output1 = Ash Battlestaff
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Ash Battlestaff}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
It's not entirely clear why a staff helps channel magic. Druids would claim that it was because they were working with nature, aligning the magic spell through the plant's systems. Members of the wizard's guild argued that it wasn't that the wood itself was special but that they worked as a focus, helping to channel the mind of the wizard rather than the wood being special.

The inclusion of rune essence in a staff's design is a testament to the meticulous craftsmanship and artistry involved. This essence, often crushed into a fine powder and dispersed throughout the wood's careful grooves, creates a magical circulatory system within the staff. In this way, the staff becomes a living entity, with the wizard as its beating heart, channelling power through its veins to unleash upon its foes. 

A staff is rarely helpful for less violent magic. They are brute force weapons, where magic is forced into elemental blasts, obliterating the target. More subtle spells rely on the caster's will and are powered directly by their magical skill. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
An ash shortbow is the first weapon most young archers train with. It’s easy to make and, therefore, not so problematic if it breaks during training. Human villagers would often craft their first bows together under the tutelage of a visiting Wild Knight. They would use branches that had naturally fallen from the trees and carefully whittle them into the bow’s limbs. Bowstrings would be created by gathering flax from the nearby fields and spinning them into thin but hardy threads. 

Most would break on first use. Some would break before the first arrow was fired. Still, village youngsters would laugh at their misfortunes and start the whole process again. Each time improving. Each new bow sturdier than the last.  
}}

Where the mithril crossbow is the standard issue of the ranged Black Knight, the black sword is standard for footsoldiers. 

'Black', you may be aware, is not a material. It is instead a descriptor for steel that has gone through a process similar to vulcanisation. There was a time when the vulcanisation was performed by the dragon Xikotal, as a gift to the isolationist knights.

With his death, the process moved towards the demonic. The resulting quality is lower, which has made the original, dragonfire 'black' metal sought after in Dowdun Reach.
}}

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Blightwood Battlestaff}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The stranger the wood, the more powerful the magic. It’s unsure why this is, but it’s been true since magic was first discovered. When used as a tool for destructive magic, Garou druids learned the power of blightwood. 

Ashien, a legendary druid among the garou, was among the first to wield such a weapon. As the story goes, they were wandering the swamps when the goddess Lougrim came to her in a vision and gave her a quest. Ashien was to journey deep into the swamps and do battle with a demon that dwelled within. 

They found the demon; it emerged out of the fog with a chilling screech and destroyed their staff with a single swing of its blade-like claw. In panic, Ashien blindly reached for the nearest weapon, the root of a swamp tree that had fallen in the mud. Through it, they channeled their will to survive, and it burst free of the branch as a flash of lightning. 

The creature burned beneath Ashien’s gaze, vanishing back into the mists as if it never were. 

Since then, garou druids would favour blightwood when crafting a staff. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The wild creatures of Ashenfall have always been elusive. Bramblemead villagers would tell wild stories about the deer that got away or the sheep that shrugged off an arrow. Still, those who could wield a bow rarely left their family hungry. 

One Wild Knight, Frode, was so skilled with the bow that he never returned from a hunt empty-handed. His siblings among the Wild Knights would send him out hunting before every great gathering so that they might feast heartily upon his return. For years, this was their happiest tradition. 

Until the day he did not return. 

It is said that his body was found in five separate places, spread out across the forests of Brynmoor like some gruesome compass. His quiver was empty, yet none of his arrows – marked as they were, with his symbol, an owl – would ever be found. 
}}

===Recipe tree===

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Every hunter reaches a crossroads in life when a choice becomes clear. They have honed their skills with a bow, surpassing the challenges posed by the Brynmoor prey. They could settle into a comfortable life in the village—hunting the same old deer, earning a regular wage, and basking in a relaxing level of respect from their peers. Or, they could dare to dream bigger, to push their skills to new heights. 

The Wild Knights journeyed into more dangerous territories, facing off against deadly monsters warped by the anima twist. Joining them would mean abandoning the comfortable life of friends and family but earning a chance to face challenges that they would never face at home. 

Most choose comfort, as one might expect. But those who choose to improve would be taught how to craft a more powerful weapon, casting aside the oak bows that carry their names. They would carve a new name and identity into the limbs of their latest weapon and join the knights. Their names would be whispered, and gossip about them would transform into fireside stories—ghost tales about those still alive—the first embers of legends. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.text:

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Blurite Crossbow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
For centuries alchemists have yearned to create gold. To transmute base metals into something pure and shiny. While gold is the goal, there are other, stranger metals, along the way, and what could be more bizarre than a bar of purest blue.

Blurite is defined by its colour. It’s in the very name. Yet it offers no real magical, or alchemical value beyond other metals. Artistic, perhaps, it was favoured by poets, bards and artisans of more eccentric tastes, but it still offered little in the way of superior weaponry.

That was until Gothren Actillian took a crack at it. He was an blacksmith, obsessed with the less desired metals and materials. He would hunt for hours in the darkest caverns for slivers of blue, ignoring glittering silver and gold. To him the metal was special, radiant and beautiful. The hues generated a joy in him that he could not describe, and he longed to craft it into something befitting its splendour. He was delighted when he found the ore glittering away in a hidden dragonkin vault in Ghornfell's central region. 

He was never any good with a sword, and his magical prowess wouldn’t frighten a kebbit. Yet crossbows, those he could wield. He was a skilled marksman, and could knock an apple from a chinchompa’s head before it exploded.

The crossbow, blue as the ocean, caused much mockery from his companions, yet their mockery soon died as he speared a bolt through the heart of a dragon. Something all of their weapons had failed to do.

He was just disappointed that the dragon’s scales were green and went searching for that ever elusive blue.
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Animal bones are surprisingly durable, especially those found on Ashenfall. The magic of the continent seeps into their marrow, slowly strengthening them and making them tougher. This goes some way to explaining the ferocity of ordinarily docile creatures, but it also helps when crafting tools. 

Cows and similar beasts were celebrated in the age of Guthix. The people of Brynmoor preferred to take an animal’s life only on the hunt or after long years of service. When an animal passed, it was thanked for its sacrifice, and the materials helped strengthen the village. 

A club forged of bone was a simple tool but one that made defending the village much easier. Roving beasts were easily pushed back, and the village was protected by the animal’s last gifts. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

===Recipe tree===

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Before he learned to use the bow, Ulv survived with simple daggers of bone. He lived outside the village, cast out for his silence and strange ways. Ulv did not follow the polite nonsense of human society, nor did he seem to care for their troubles. The boy was happy just to survive and whittle small carvings of animals from fallen branches. 

He hunted silently, using the daggers to slay small prey such as rabbits and chickens. He ate what he needed and sold the excess at the markets for enough coin to purchase simple clothes and tools. 

When he was thirteen, he was beaten by bandits and left for dead. But a Wild Knight found him and brought him to safety. Recovering in the knight’s home, Ulv would be taught how to use a bow, a skill he soon mastered. 

When a wolf attacked Bramblemead, this outcast slew it. Villagers thanked him for his bravery and asked him what he wanted as a reward. He refuted their offers and asked only for the wolf’s skull in return. He fashioned it into a mask that he chose as a new face, one he wore away from the village that had spurned him. 
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

pages.text:

===Recipe tree===

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
We are made of sturdier materials than we give ourselves credit for. The human body can withstand a staggering force before our bones give way to the stresses. So too is it for the bones of animals, especially those of Ashenfall. Here, the magic seeps into the marrow and strengthens the bone to be sturdier than before.

When used as tools, refined into weapons, these bones can be made to break stone and release metal ore from its prison.  
}}

pages.text:
{{Infobox Item
|name = Bronze Crossbow
|image = Bronze Crossbow.png
|type = Ranged Weapon
|description = A 1-Handed Crossbow made from Bronze.
|weight = 10.0
}}
'''Bronze Crossbow''' is a weapon item.

==Stats==
{{Infobox Weapon
| power = 3
| durability = 1050
| damagetype = Ranged
| attackstyle = Crossbow
| damagemulti = 1.4
}}
[[File:Bronze Crossbow (Equipped).jpg|thumb|An Bronze Crossbow as seen when equipped by a player]]

==Recipe==
{{Recipe
|facility = Fletching Bench
|skill = Artisan
|skillxp = 150
|mat1 = Oak Logs
|mat1qty = 12
|mat2 = Wool Thread
|mat2qty = 6
|mat3 = Bronze Crossbow Limbs
|mat3qty = 1
|mat4 = Opal
|mat4qty = 1
|output1 = Bronze Crossbow
}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bronze Crossbow}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The members of the Wild Knights who trained with melee weapons, generally found themselves the victors in friendly sparring matches against their companions. The reasoning was simple – shields. A shield could turn aside blade, arrow and spell, with equal skill and it left their enemy vulnerable to counterattack. This sense of superiority began to change from friendly competition, into a sense of smugness capable of souring many friendships.

Roliker Hallister was particularly obnoxious. His skill with sword and shield was admittedly impressive. At the age of twenty he could best even some of the commanders of the Wild Knights in single combat. Healthy pride twisted into smug egoism, which then rotted down into unpleasant superiority. He was rude. He was dismissive. He was pretty much an obnoxious nightmare to be around.

Which is why he was very surprised by the bolt in his shoulder, and why no one else seemed too inclined to help him out. Vanida Mox was a skilled archer and inventor. She was quick to learn, quick to study and she had the enviable ability to see when something was wrong and work to find a solution. Popular with the other knights, because she would work with them, share accolades, and stand for what the order truly meant – a barrier between the world and the wild.

As Roliker’s ego grew and grew, she realised that the archers in the order were at a significant disadvantage in close combat. At range, they were obviously superior, but as all the sparring matches were held in the courtyard, there was precious little distance available. So she began working on a device that would deliver the power of an arrow but allowing one hand free to hold a shield.

It was very effective.

She named it a “cross-bow”, because she was very very angry when she unloaded it into the cloud of smugness known as Roliker. Later that name would be attributes to the mechanical structure of the device, but now you know better.
}}

<!-- Generated by generate_item_pages.py -->

|mat3 = Wool Thread
|mat3qty = 1

}}

===Recipe tree===
{{Crafting Tree|Bronze Dagger}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Bronze is a common enough alloy, a combination of copper and tin. Durable, defiant, and deadly in the right hands. For Mia Larsdottir, it was a bronze dagger that pierced her heart, cutting her life short at the age of twenty-one. 

As the first of the Amalgamated, she had never expected to reach any of the milestones her peers traditionally followed. She did not expect to be married, and she never let herself dream of being a mother. She thought, perhaps, that she might be allowed to carve a quiet little place for herself, somewhere with room for cats. 

But a bronze dagger stole that dream from her. 

Her killer was never brought to justice, though there were rumours about who they were. Her father, being the chief suspect, ashamed as he was to bring the first of the unnatural Amalgamated into the world. He denied it, of course, but he never held a funeral for her. Instead, her body was left in the forest, where nature might put her at peace. 
}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
The Wild Knight Kessel was unusual in her choice of weaponry. Where most of the order chose to wield bows, daggers, or swift blades, Kessel favoured a bronze greatsword. On the battlefield, she hefted it across her shoulders, never letting the hilt leave her grip.  

As one of the Amalgamated, Kessel’s skin glowed like burning coals, her battle scars resembling faint reddish embers. She harnessed this heat, channelling it through her sword. Her magic set the blade ablaze, a sight that struck terror into the hearts of her enemies. With great arcs of red flame, she cleaved through enemy forces, incinerating them as she severed their limbs from their bodies. 

On one fateful day, she would use this technique against a force of garou hunters. The battle was long, hard, and brutal, and both sides faced great casualties. But it was when she sliced her blade across the young garou prince that war was inevitable.
}}

{{Crafting Tree|Bronze Logging Axe}}

==Journal==
{{Journal entry|
Where bone and stone fail, metal provides. The bronze axe is strong enough to withstand greater force than its predecessors and can break through the bark of some of the strongest trees. On Ashenfall, nature has been hardened by the ambient magic, the bark of trees makes them resistant to most attacks. Yet, the bronze axe breaks through that barrier, not by magic, nor by cunning, but by sheer durability and resolve.

The lumberjacks of Brynmoor favoured bronze axes over iron. While iron was stronger and felled a tree quicker, the relatively cheapness and availability of copper and tin ore, made it a much more cost effective choice.

The villagers disapproved when the leadership pushed for iron in everything. They felt that bronze was more than enough for the village needs. But the lord wanted progress, and didn’t care about the price
}}
